summary: Risk-based due diligence on correspondent banking relationships including initial assessment, periodic reviews, and ongoing transaction monitoring for BSA/AML compliance.
description:
This process governs the establishment and ongoing oversight of correspondent banking relationships, which are among the highest-risk relationships a financial institution maintains. It covers initial due diligence questionnaire collection, FATF jurisdiction risk assessment, respondent bank AML program evaluation, nested account and downstream correspondent analysis, and periodic re-certification. The process implements FinCEN's guidance on correspondent accounts for foreign financial institutions under Section 312 of the USA PATRIOT Act and addresses de-risking considerations per OCC and Federal Reserve supervisory expectations.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Due diligence completion rate: 100% before relationship activation
- Periodic review cycle adherence: 100% of annual/biennial reviews completed on schedule
- Transaction monitoring alert disposition: < 5 business days average for correspondent-related alerts
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- Section 312 USA PATRIOT Act: EDD required for all foreign correspondent accounts
- Shell bank prohibition: must obtain certification that respondent is not a shell bank per 31 CFR 1010.630
- FinCEN 314(a) and 314(b) information sharing obligations for correspondent-related investigations

### Risk Framework
- Correspondent relationships must be tiered: standard risk (annual review), elevated risk (semi-annual), high risk (quarterly)
- Nested correspondent and payable-through accounts require identification of downstream institutions
- Transaction monitoring rules must include correspondent-specific typologies (e.g., funnel accounts, rapid movement of funds)

### SLA & Performance
- Initial due diligence: completed within 30 business days of relationship request
- Annual re-certification: completed within 60 calendar days of anniversary date
- Suspicious activity escalation: referred to BSA team within 24 hours of detection
human_approvals:
### New Relationship Approval
- **Trigger**: Completed due diligence package assembled for new correspondent banking relationship
- **Reviewer**: Correspondent Banking Risk Committee
- **Actions**: Approve relationship with standard monitoring, approve with enhanced monitoring and transaction limits, decline relationship
- **Timeout**: 72h → escalate to Chief Risk Officer

### Elevated Risk Findings
- **Trigger**: Periodic review identifies material deficiencies in respondent's AML program or jurisdiction downgraded by FATF
- **Reviewer**: BSA Officer and Head of Correspondent Banking
- **Actions**: Issue remediation requirements with 90-day deadline, restrict transaction types and volumes, recommend relationship exit
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Risk Committee

### Relationship Exit Decision
- **Trigger**: Respondent bank fails to remediate identified deficiencies or multiple SARs filed on relationship
- **Reviewer**: Senior Management Committee (BSA Officer, CRO, Head of International Banking)
- **Actions**: Approve orderly wind-down with 180-day notice, extend remediation period with enhanced controls, continue with board-level risk acceptance
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to Board of Directors

name: Commercial Underwriting & Risk Assessment
summary: Commercial lines underwriting process encompassing risk evaluation, hazard analysis, pricing, terms negotiation, and policy issuance for property, casualty, and specialty lines.
description:
This process manages the end-to-end underwriting workflow for commercial insurance submissions across property, general liability, commercial auto, workers' compensation, and umbrella/excess lines. It begins with submission intake from brokers, proceeds through risk assessment including loss history analysis, hazard identification, financial stability review, and industry benchmarking. Pricing is developed using actuarial models, experience rating, and schedule rating modifications. The process handles quote generation, terms negotiation with brokers, binder issuance, and policy document generation. Multi-line accounts require coordinated underwriting across divisions.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Submission-to-quote turnaround: < 5 business days for standard risks, < 10 for complex
- Quote-to-bind ratio: > 35% overall, > 50% for renewal business
- Loss ratio for new business: within 5 points of actuarial target by underwriting year
business_constraints:
### Underwriting Authority
- Individual underwriter binding authority limits defined by line of business and experience level
- Multi-line accounts require sign-off from each line underwriter plus account coordinator
- Accounts in admitted markets must comply with filed rates and forms per state insurance regulations

### Risk Appetite
- Maximum probable loss (MPL) per location cannot exceed 10% of treaty capacity without facultative reinsurance
- Minimum premium thresholds apply per line of business; accounts below threshold directed to program business
- Prohibited classes: cannabis operations, cryptocurrency mining, PFAS manufacturing (unless specialty division approved)

### SLA & Performance
- Broker submission acknowledgment: within 4 business hours
- Standard quote delivery: 5 business days from complete submission
- Policy issuance: within 10 business days of binding
human_approvals:
### Large Account Authority
- **Trigger**: Total account premium exceeds $500K or any single line exceeds underwriter's binding authority
- **Reviewer**: Underwriting Manager or VP of Underwriting
- **Actions**: Approve within authority, approve with modifications to terms/pricing, decline risk
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to Chief Underwriting Officer

### Adverse Loss History Override
- **Trigger**: Account loss ratio exceeds 75% over trailing 5 years or single loss exceeds $1M
- **Reviewer**: Senior Underwriter and Actuarial Analyst
- **Actions**: Approve with rate surcharge and deductible increase, approve with loss control requirements, decline to quote
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Underwriting Manager

### Unusual Risk Acceptance
- **Trigger**: Submission involves excluded hazard, sanctioned territory, or class of business outside appetite
- **Reviewer**: Chief Underwriting Officer
- **Actions**: Grant exception with documented rationale and reinsurance confirmation, refer to specialty division, decline risk
- **Timeout**: 72h → auto-decline with explanation to broker

### Pricing Deviation Approval
- **Trigger**: Proposed premium deviates more than 15% below actuarial indicated rate
- **Reviewer**: Actuarial Manager and Underwriting Manager
- **Actions**: Approve with documented competitive justification, adjust to minimum acceptable rate, decline to match competitor
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to VP of Underwriting

name: Policy Administration & Lifecycle Management
summary: Full policy servicing lifecycle including endorsement processing, premium adjustments, renewal management, cancellations, and reinstatements across all lines of business.
description:
This process manages the post-issuance servicing of insurance policies throughout their lifecycle. It handles mid-term endorsements (adding/removing coverages, locations, vehicles, or named insureds), premium audits for adjustable-rate policies, renewal processing with updated loss experience and rating, cancellation processing (insured-requested and carrier-initiated), and reinstatement workflows. The process must maintain accurate policy records, generate compliant documentation, and ensure premium accounting accuracy. It supports both commercial and personal lines across multiple state jurisdictions with varying regulatory requirements.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Endorsement processing time: < 3 business days from request to policy update
- Renewal retention rate: > 85% for profitable book segments
- Premium accounting accuracy: 99.5% of transactions posted correctly on first pass
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Compliance
- Cancellation notices must comply with state-specific statutory notice periods (10-60 days depending on jurisdiction and reason)
- Non-renewal notices must be sent within state-mandated timeframes (30-120 days prior to expiration)
- All endorsements and policy documents must use state-approved forms and filings

### Premium Accounting
- Earned/unearned premium calculations must follow the policy's earning pattern (pro-rata or short-rate)
- Return premiums on cancellation must be issued within 15 business days per most state requirements
- Commission adjustments must post concurrently with premium changes

### SLA & Performance
- Standard endorsements: processed within 3 business days
- Renewal offers: delivered 60 days prior to policy expiration
- Certificate of insurance requests: issued within 4 business hours
human_approvals:
### Material Coverage Change
- **Trigger**: Endorsement adds new coverage line, increases limits by more than 50%, or adds high-hazard exposure
- **Reviewer**: Underwriter of record
- **Actions**: Approve endorsement with updated pricing, approve with subjectivities, decline change and offer alternative
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to Underwriting Manager

### Carrier-Initiated Cancellation
- **Trigger**: Non-payment after grace period, material misrepresentation discovered, or unacceptable risk condition found at inspection
- **Reviewer**: Underwriting Manager and Compliance Officer
- **Actions**: Issue cancellation notice per state statutory requirements, offer conditional reinstatement, refer to special handling
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to VP of Underwriting (statutory notice period at risk)

### Premium Audit Dispute
- **Trigger**: Insured disputes premium audit findings with variance exceeding $10K or 20% of estimated premium
- **Reviewer**: Premium Audit Supervisor
- **Actions**: Accept insured's documentation and adjust, order re-audit, escalate to arbitration
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to Controller

name: Reinsurance Treaty Management
summary: Reinsurance program lifecycle including treaty and facultative placement, premium and loss cession processing, bordereaux reporting, and settlement accounting.
description:
This process manages the end-to-end reinsurance operations for a primary insurer's ceded reinsurance program. It covers treaty renewal negotiations and placement through brokers, facultative certificate placement for individual risks exceeding treaty capacity, premium cession calculations and bordereau reporting, loss recovery notifications and collection, and quarterly/annual accounting settlements. The process coordinates between ceding company underwriters, reinsurance managers, actuaries, brokers, and reinsurer counterparties. It must ensure accurate cession accounting under US GAAP/SSAP and maintain reinsurance recoverables in compliance with NAIC credit-for-reinsurance requirements.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Treaty renewal placement: completed 30 days prior to inception
- Bordereaux submission accuracy: > 99% of cessions reported correctly
- Reinsurance recoverable collection: > 95% collected within 90 days of billing
business_constraints:
### Regulatory & Accounting
- Credit for reinsurance per NAIC Model Law: authorized reinsurers must maintain required collateral or certification
- SSAP 62R compliance for prospective and retroactive reinsurance accounting treatment
- Schedule F reporting must be accurate for all ceded reinsurance in statutory annual statement

### Counterparty Risk
- Reinsurer minimum financial strength rating: A.M. Best A- (or equivalent S&P/Moody's) unless collateralized
- Single reinsurer concentration limit: no more than 20% of total ceded premium to any one counterparty
- Collateral requirements for non-admitted or below-threshold reinsurers per state regulations

### SLA & Performance
- Quarterly bordereaux submitted within 45 days of quarter-end
- Loss recovery notifications sent to reinsurers within 5 business days of paid loss exceeding treaty retention
- Treaty settlements processed within 30 days of account rendering
human_approvals:
### Treaty Terms Approval
- **Trigger**: Reinsurance broker presents final treaty terms and pricing for upcoming renewal
- **Reviewer**: VP of Reinsurance and Chief Actuary
- **Actions**: Accept terms and authorize signing, counter-propose pricing or structural modifications, explore alternative markets
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to CRO (renewal inception at risk)

### Large Facultative Placement
- **Trigger**: Individual risk requires facultative placement exceeding $10M in ceded limit
- **Reviewer**: Reinsurance Manager
- **Actions**: Approve placement with selected reinsurers, modify retention/cession structure, decline and adjust net retention
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to VP of Reinsurance

### Commutation or Novation
- **Trigger**: Reinsurer downgrade below A.M. Best A- or reinsurer requests commutation of run-off portfolio
- **Reviewer**: Reinsurance Committee (VP Reinsurance, Chief Actuary, CFO)
- **Actions**: Accept commutation at negotiated value, novate to replacement reinsurer, maintain contract with collateral requirements
- **Timeout**: 10 business days → escalate to Board Risk Committee

name: Subrogation Recovery
summary: Identification, pursuit, and collection of claim cost recoveries from responsible third parties through negotiation, arbitration, and litigation channels.
description:
This process manages the recovery of claim payments from at-fault third parties and their insurers. It begins with subrogation potential identification during claims handling, proceeds through demand letter issuance, negotiation with adverse carriers, and escalation to inter-company arbitration (Arbitration Forums) or litigation when necessary. The process tracks recovery on both open and closed claims, manages deductible reimbursement to insureds, and ensures compliance with made-whole doctrine and anti-subrogation rules by jurisdiction. Recovery performance directly impacts the company's net loss ratio and combined ratio.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Subrogation identification rate: > 25% of applicable claim types flagged for recovery
- Recovery rate: > 60% of identified subrogation value collected
- Average time to recovery: < 120 days from demand to collection
business_constraints:
### Legal Requirements
- Made-whole doctrine: insured must be fully compensated before carrier retains subrogation recovery (varies by state)
- Statute of limitations tracked per jurisdiction and cause of action (typically 2-6 years)
- Anti-subrogation rules: no subrogation against co-insureds or additional insureds under the same policy

### Financial Thresholds
- Minimum recovery pursuit threshold: $1,000 (below this amount, cost-benefit analysis required)
- Litigation cost-benefit: projected recovery must exceed 2x estimated legal costs to proceed
- Deductible reimbursement: insured's deductible repaid from first dollars recovered

### SLA & Performance
- Subrogation demand issued within 30 days of claim payment
- Arbitration Forums filing within 120 days of demand non-response
- Recovery funds applied to claim within 5 business days of receipt
human_approvals:
### Litigation Referral
- **Trigger**: Adverse carrier denies liability or demand exceeds $50K with no response within 60 days
- **Reviewer**: Subrogation Manager
- **Actions**: Refer to panel counsel, proceed to Arbitration Forums, write off recovery as uneconomical
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to Claims Director

### Settlement Authority
- **Trigger**: Adverse carrier offers settlement at less than 80% of demanded amount or recovery exceeds $100K
- **Reviewer**: Senior Subrogation Specialist or Subrogation Manager (based on amount)
- **Actions**: Accept settlement, counter-offer with documented rationale, reject and proceed to arbitration
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Claims Director

### Recovery Write-Off
- **Trigger**: Recovery pursuit deemed uneconomical (cost to pursue exceeds expected recovery) or statute of limitations approaching
- **Reviewer**: Subrogation Manager
- **Actions**: Approve write-off with documented rationale, make final demand attempt, refer to collection vendor
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to VP of Claims

name: Workers' Compensation Claims Management
summary: Workers' compensation claim intake, medical treatment management, disability determination, return-to-work coordination, and settlement for occupational injuries and illnesses.
description:
This process manages the full lifecycle of workers' compensation claims from first report of injury through claim closure. It encompasses employer notification and claim intake, compensability investigation, medical treatment authorization and bill review, temporary and permanent disability benefit calculation, vocational rehabilitation referral, and return-to-work coordination. The process must comply with jurisdiction-specific workers' compensation statutes covering benefit rates, medical fee schedules, and dispute resolution procedures. For complex claims, it coordinates independent medical examinations, Medicare Set-Aside allocations, and structured settlement negotiations.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Three-point contact completion: 100% within 24 hours of claim assignment (claimant, employer, treating physician)
- Average claim duration: < 45 days for medical-only claims, < 180 days for indemnity claims
- Return-to-work rate: > 85% of claimants returned to work within 90 days
business_constraints:
### Statutory Requirements
- Initial benefit payments must commence within statutory timeframes (7-21 days depending on jurisdiction)
- Compensability determination must be made within state-mandated investigation period (14-90 days by jurisdiction)
- All medical treatment must be authorized under applicable state medical fee schedule and treatment guidelines

### Medical Management
- Utilization review decisions must be communicated within state-mandated timeframes (typically 5 business days prospective, 30 days retrospective)
- Independent medical examinations limited per state statute (typically 1-2 per claim without court order)
- Prescription drug management must follow state formulary requirements where applicable

### SLA & Performance
- First report of injury processed and claim created within 4 hours of receipt
- Three-point contact completed within 24 hours of claim assignment
- Indemnity benefit calculations completed and payments initiated within 3 business days of compensability determination
human_approvals:
### Compensability Determination
- **Trigger**: Investigation reveals potential for claim denial (pre-existing condition, intoxication, horseplay, or employment status dispute)
- **Reviewer**: Senior Claims Examiner
- **Actions**: Accept claim as compensable, deny with detailed rationale and statutory citation, request additional investigation
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to Claims Supervisor (statutory acceptance deadline at risk)

### Surgery Authorization
- **Trigger**: Treating physician requests surgical intervention or treatment exceeding $25K
- **Reviewer**: Nurse Case Manager and Medical Director
- **Actions**: Authorize treatment, request peer review or IME, deny and propose alternative treatment plan
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Medical Director (utilization review timeline applies)

### Settlement Authority
- **Trigger**: Claim identified for compromise and release settlement or Medicare Set-Aside required
- **Reviewer**: Claims Manager (up to $250K), VP of Claims (above $250K)
- **Actions**: Approve settlement authority, adjust reserve and settlement range, decline settlement and continue claim management
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to Chief Claims Officer

### Fraud Referral
- **Trigger**: Surveillance, social media investigation, or medical records reveal potential claimant or provider fraud
- **Reviewer**: Special Investigations Unit Manager
- **Actions**: Open SIU investigation, refer to state fraud bureau, deny claim with documented evidence
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to VP of Claims and Legal

name: Commercial Lease Administration
summary: CRE lease lifecycle management including lease abstracting, rent and CAM billing, escalation tracking, renewal negotiations, and annual operating expense reconciliation.
description:
This process manages the administration of commercial real estate leases across office, retail, and industrial portfolios. It covers lease abstracting and critical date tracking, monthly base rent and additional rent (CAM, insurance, taxes) billing, percentage rent calculations for retail tenants, annual operating expense reconciliation and true-up billing, lease renewal and expansion negotiations, and tenant move-in/move-out coordination. The process ensures compliance with lease terms, GAAP/ASC 842 lease accounting requirements, and local real property laws. It serves property managers, asset managers, accounting teams, and leasing brokers across multi-property portfolios.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Rent collection rate: > 97% within 10 days of due date
- CAM reconciliation completion: within 90 days of fiscal year-end for all properties
- Critical date management: zero missed renewal option or termination notice deadlines
business_constraints:
### Lease Compliance
- All rent escalations (CPI, fixed, market reset) must be calculated and applied per lease terms on anniversary dates
- Operating expense pass-throughs must comply with lease-defined inclusions, exclusions, and caps (gross-up provisions for vacancy)
- Percentage rent breakpoint calculations must be audited quarterly for retail tenants

### Accounting Standards
- ASC 842 lease classification and disclosure requirements must be maintained for all lessor accounting
- Straight-line rent recognition for leases with free rent periods or escalating rent schedules
- Tenant improvement allowance amortization must follow GAAP treatment per lease classification

### SLA & Performance
- Monthly rent invoices delivered by the 1st of each month
- CAM reconciliation statements delivered within 90 days of fiscal year-end
- Tenant maintenance requests responded to within 4 business hours, resolved within 48 hours
human_approvals:
### Lease Renewal Decision
- **Trigger**: Lease expiration approaching within 12 months and tenant has not exercised renewal option
- **Reviewer**: Asset Manager and Leasing Director
- **Actions**: Propose renewal terms to tenant, market space for re-leasing, offer short-term extension pending market analysis
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to VP of Asset Management

### CAM Reconciliation Dispute
- **Trigger**: Tenant disputes annual CAM reconciliation with variance claim exceeding $10K or 15% of estimated charges
- **Reviewer**: Senior Property Accountant and Property Manager
- **Actions**: Provide detailed expense documentation, adjust charges per audit findings, offer tenant ledger credit
- **Timeout**: 10 business days → escalate to Controller

### Rent Abatement or Concession
- **Trigger**: Tenant requests rent relief citing building condition issue, co-tenancy clause trigger, or financial hardship
- **Reviewer**: Asset Manager
- **Actions**: Approve temporary abatement with recovery terms, deny with lease citation, negotiate modified terms
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Portfolio Manager

### Lease Default Notice
- **Trigger**: Tenant delinquent beyond 30 days or in material breach of lease covenants
- **Reviewer**: Property Manager and Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Issue notice to cure, initiate eviction proceedings, negotiate workout agreement
- **Timeout**: 24h → escalate to VP of Property Management (cure period statutory deadlines apply)

name: Property Management Operations
summary: Multi-property operational management covering preventive and reactive maintenance, property inspections, vendor coordination, tenant relations, and regulatory compliance.
description:
This process oversees the day-to-day operations of a diversified real estate portfolio spanning residential, commercial, and mixed-use properties. It covers work order management for reactive and preventive maintenance, scheduled property inspections and condition assessments, vendor procurement and performance management, tenant communication and complaint resolution, and regulatory compliance including fire/life safety, ADA accessibility, and environmental requirements. The process integrates capital expenditure planning with ongoing maintenance to optimize property condition and NOI performance.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Work order completion: > 90% of non-emergency requests resolved within 48 hours
- Preventive maintenance compliance: > 95% of scheduled tasks completed on time
- Tenant satisfaction score: > 4.2 out of 5.0 on quarterly surveys
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Compliance
- Fire/life safety inspections must comply with NFPA codes and local fire marshal requirements (annual minimum)
- ADA accessibility standards must be maintained for all common areas and public accommodations
- Environmental compliance including asbestos management plans, lead paint disclosures, and stormwater management

### Operational Standards
- Preferred vendor list must be maintained with current insurance certificates, W-9s, and performance ratings
- All vendors must carry minimum insurance: $1M general liability, $1M auto, statutory workers' comp
- Preventive maintenance schedules must follow manufacturer specifications for all critical building systems

### SLA & Performance
- Emergency maintenance response: on-site within 2 hours, 24/7/365
- Routine work orders: acknowledged within 4 hours, completed within 48 hours
- Common area cleanliness and landscaping inspections: weekly with documented checklists
human_approvals:
### Emergency Repair Authorization
- **Trigger**: Emergency maintenance issue (HVAC failure, water intrusion, elevator outage, fire/life safety) with estimated cost exceeding $10K
- **Reviewer**: Property Manager
- **Actions**: Authorize emergency repair, obtain competitive bids if time permits, engage preferred vendor from approved list
- **Timeout**: 2h → auto-authorize up to $25K with preferred vendor and notify Director of Operations

### Capital Expenditure Approval
- **Trigger**: Repair or replacement identified that exceeds operating budget threshold ($25K) and qualifies as capital expenditure
- **Reviewer**: Director of Operations and Asset Manager
- **Actions**: Approve and fund from capital reserve, defer to next budget cycle, request additional bids
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to VP of Asset Management

### Vendor Contract Award
- **Trigger**: New service contract exceeding $50K annually or existing contract renewal with proposed increase > 5%
- **Reviewer**: Procurement Manager and Property Manager
- **Actions**: Award contract to recommended vendor, request additional proposals, negotiate pricing reduction
- **Timeout**: 10 business days → escalate to Director of Operations

name: Real Estate Investment Analysis
summary: Acquisition underwriting and investment analysis including DCF modeling, cap rate analysis, market comparables, physical and environmental due diligence, and investment committee presentation.
description:
This process governs the evaluation and approval of commercial real estate acquisition opportunities from initial screening through investment committee approval and closing. It covers deal sourcing and preliminary screening against investment criteria, financial modeling including discounted cash flow (DCF), direct capitalization, and internal rate of return (IRR) analysis, market research and comparable sales analysis, physical due diligence (property condition assessments, environmental Phase I/II), legal due diligence (title, survey, zoning, entitlements), and investment committee memorandum preparation. The process supports acquisitions across core, value-add, and opportunistic strategies for office, industrial, multifamily, and retail asset classes.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Deal screening-to-LOI conversion: > 15% of screened opportunities advance to letter of intent
- Underwriting accuracy: actual Year 1 NOI within 5% of underwritten projection
- Due diligence completion: all workstreams completed within 45-day inspection period
business_constraints:
### Investment Criteria
- Minimum unlevered IRR: 7.0% for core, 12.0% for value-add, 18.0% for opportunistic acquisitions
- Maximum loan-to-value: 65% for core, 75% for value-add (recourse limited to equity commitment)
- Target cash-on-cash return: minimum 5.0% stabilized yield for income-focused strategies

### Due Diligence Requirements
- Phase I Environmental Site Assessment required for all acquisitions per ASTM E1527-21
- Property Condition Assessment with capital expenditure projections for a 12-year hold period
- Title insurance commitment with survey review; all exceptions must be reviewed by legal counsel

### SLA & Performance
- Preliminary underwriting completed within 5 business days of receiving offering memorandum
- LOI submitted within 2 business days of authorization
- Investment memorandum distributed to committee 5 business days prior to scheduled meeting
human_approvals:
### Letter of Intent Authorization
- **Trigger**: Preliminary underwriting meets return thresholds and deal team recommends pursuing acquisition
- **Reviewer**: Managing Director of Acquisitions
- **Actions**: Authorize LOI submission at recommended terms, adjust pricing/terms and resubmit, pass on opportunity
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to Chief Investment Officer (competitive bid deadline risk)

### Investment Committee Approval
- **Trigger**: Due diligence substantially complete and final underwriting packaged in investment memorandum
- **Reviewer**: Investment Committee (CIO, CFO, Head of Acquisitions, Head of Asset Management)
- **Actions**: Approve acquisition at recommended terms, approve with conditions (price adjustment, additional reserves), decline acquisition
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to CEO (inspection period expiration risk)

### Environmental Risk Acceptance
- **Trigger**: Phase I ESA identifies recognized environmental conditions (RECs) or Phase II confirms contamination
- **Reviewer**: Environmental Counsel and Managing Director of Acquisitions
- **Actions**: Accept risk with negotiated indemnification and remediation escrow, require seller remediation prior to closing, withdraw from acquisition
- **Timeout**: 72h → escalate to General Counsel

### Financing Approval
- **Trigger**: Loan term sheet received from lender with final terms for acquisition financing
- **Reviewer**: CFO and Treasury Director
- **Actions**: Accept financing terms, negotiate modifications, seek alternative lender
- **Timeout**: 48h → escalate to CIO (rate lock expiration risk)

name: Prior Authorization Processing
summary: Insurance prior authorization for medical procedures and specialty medications with payer-specific rules and clinical criteria.
description:
A healthcare prior authorization process handling 3,000-8,000 authorization requests per month across inpatient procedures, outpatient surgeries, advanced imaging, and specialty pharmaceuticals. The process integrates with payer portals, clinical guidelines (InterQual/MCG), and EHR systems to streamline submissions and reduce denials.

**Key performance metrics:**
- First-pass approval rate: >75%
- Average turnaround: <48 hours for standard, <24 hours for urgent
- Denial overturn rate on appeal: >60%
- Clean submission rate: >90%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **CMS Medicare Advantage** prior auth rules and timeframes
- **State prompt-decision statutes** (varies by jurisdiction, typically 2-15 business days)
- **HIPAA** compliance for all PHI exchange with payers
- **No Surprises Act** protections for emergency services

### Business Rules
- All requests must include **valid ICD-10 diagnosis** and **CPT/HCPCS procedure codes**
- Urgent requests must be submitted within **24 hours** of clinical determination
- Denied requests eligible for **two levels of appeal** before external review
- Retro-authorization requests accepted within **72 hours** of emergency service

### SLA & Performance
- Standard requests: decision within **5 business days**
- Urgent requests: decision within **24 hours**
- Appeal submissions: within **30 days** of denial notification
human_approvals:
### Urgent/Emergent Override
- **Trigger**: Request marked urgent or emergent by ordering physician
- **Reviewer**: Utilization Review Nurse
- **Actions**: Validate clinical urgency, expedite payer submission, arrange concurrent review
- **Timeout**: 4 hours → escalate to Medical Director

### Peer-to-Peer Review
- **Trigger**: Initial authorization denied by payer
- **Reviewer**: Attending Physician + Payer Medical Director
- **Actions**: Present clinical rationale, provide additional documentation, negotiate approval
- **Timeout**: 48 hours → formal appeal submission

### High-Cost Procedure Approval
- **Trigger**: Estimated cost > $50,000 OR experimental/investigational classification
- **Reviewer**: Medical Director + Finance Review
- **Actions**: Validate medical necessity, review alternatives, confirm financial responsibility
- **Timeout**: 72 hours → escalate to CMO

name: Medical Credentialing & Privileging
summary: Provider credentialing and hospital privileging with primary source verification and NCQA compliance.
description:
A medical staff credentialing process managing initial appointments, reappointments, and privilege delineation for 500-2,000 providers across a multi-facility health system. The process ensures compliance with NCQA credentialing standards, Joint Commission requirements, and state medical board regulations through primary source verification of education, training, licensure, and malpractice history.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Average credentialing cycle: <60 days (initial), <90 days (reappointment)
- Primary source verification completion: 100%
- NCQA compliance score: >95%
- Provider satisfaction: >85%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **NCQA Credentialing Standards** for managed care compliance
- **Joint Commission** medical staff standards (MS chapters)
- **NPDB** reporting of adverse actions within 30 days
- **State medical practice acts** and licensing board requirements

### Business Rules
- All providers must have **current, unrestricted license** in state of practice
- **Primary source verification** required for: education, training, licensure, board certification, DEA
- Reappointment cycle: every **2 years** with performance data review
- Temporary privileges limited to **120 days** maximum

### SLA & Performance
- Initial credentialing completed within **60 days** of complete application
- Reappointment files prepared **90 days** before expiration
- NPDB query at initial and every **2 years** thereafter
human_approvals:
### Credentials Committee Review
- **Trigger**: Initial appointment OR adverse information discovered during verification
- **Reviewer**: Credentials Committee (physician-led)
- **Actions**: Review application, verify qualifications, evaluate references, recommend privileges
- **Timeout**: 30 days → expedited committee session

### Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE)
- **Trigger**: New privilege granted OR concern raised about competency
- **Reviewer**: Department Chair + Quality Committee
- **Actions**: Define evaluation criteria, monitor cases, review outcomes, determine ongoing privileges
- **Timeout**: 6-month evaluation period

### Adverse Action Review
- **Trigger**: Recommendation to deny, restrict, or revoke privileges
- **Reviewer**: Medical Executive Committee + Fair Hearing Panel
- **Actions**: Due process hearing, evidence review, final determination, NPDB reporting
- **Timeout**: 30 days for hearing request → proceed with action

name: Patient Discharge Planning
summary: Coordinated patient discharge with care transition planning, follow-up scheduling, and readmission prevention.
description:
A hospital discharge planning process coordinating safe transitions for 1,500-4,000 discharges per month across acute care, surgical, and observation units. The process integrates case management, social work, pharmacy, and community resources to ensure patients have appropriate post-acute care arrangements, medication reconciliation, and follow-up appointments.

**Key performance metrics:**
- 30-day readmission rate: <12%
- Discharge within 2 hours of order: >80%
- Follow-up appointment scheduled before discharge: >95%
- Patient/family education completion: 100%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **CMS Conditions of Participation** discharge planning requirements
- **EMTALA** stabilization requirements before transfer/discharge
- **Medicare Two-Midnight Rule** for observation vs. inpatient status
- **Important Message from Medicare** (IM) notice delivery

### Business Rules
- **Medication reconciliation** completed and reviewed with patient before discharge
- Patients with **high readmission risk** (LACE score >10) require enhanced follow-up plan
- **DME and home health** orders must be placed minimum **24 hours** before planned discharge
- All discharge summaries completed within **24 hours** of discharge

### SLA & Performance
- Discharge orders executed within **2 hours**
- PCP notification within **24 hours** of discharge
- Post-discharge phone call within **48 hours** for high-risk patients
human_approvals:
### Complex Discharge Review
- **Trigger**: SNF/LTACH placement needed OR homelessness OR no caregiver support
- **Reviewer**: Case Manager + Social Worker
- **Actions**: Assess post-acute needs, identify placement options, arrange transportation, coordinate insurance
- **Timeout**: 24 hours → escalate to Director of Case Management

### Against Medical Advice (AMA) Intervention
- **Trigger**: Patient requests discharge against medical recommendation
- **Reviewer**: Attending Physician + Risk Management
- **Actions**: Assess decision-making capacity, document risks explained, provide safety plan
- **Timeout**: Immediate assessment required

name: Medical Coding & Revenue Cycle
summary: HIM coding, charge capture, claim submission, and denial management for optimal reimbursement.
description:
A healthcare revenue cycle process handling medical coding (ICD-10-CM/PCS, CPT, HCPCS), charge capture validation, claim submission, and denial management for a provider organization generating 50,000-100,000 claims per month. The process spans professional and facility billing across multiple payers with a focus on coding accuracy, clean claim rates, and denial prevention.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Clean claim rate: >96%
- First-pass resolution rate: >90%
- Days in A/R: <35 days
- Net collection rate: >97%
- Coding accuracy: >95%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **HIPAA** transaction and code set standards (837P/837I)
- **False Claims Act** and Anti-Kickback Statute compliance
- **OIG Compliance Program Guidance** for coding practices
- **ICD-10 Official Guidelines** for coding and reporting

### Business Rules
- All claims must be submitted within **timely filing limits** (typically 90-365 days by payer)
- **Modifier usage** must follow CPT/CMS guidelines (no unbundling)
- Charges not posted within **3 business days** of service trigger late-charge review
- Denial write-offs over **$5,000** require Director approval

### SLA & Performance
- Inpatient coding completed within **3 business days** of discharge
- Outpatient coding completed within **2 business days** of encounter
- Claim submission within **5 business days** of coding
human_approvals:
### High-Dollar Claim Review
- **Trigger**: Claim amount > $100,000 OR DRG weight > 4.0
- **Reviewer**: Senior Coder + Revenue Integrity Analyst
- **Actions**: Validate code assignment, verify documentation supports severity, confirm charge capture
- **Timeout**: 24 hours → escalate to Coding Manager

### Denial Appeal Authorization
- **Trigger**: Payer denial on clinical grounds OR coding downgrade
- **Reviewer**: Denial Management Specialist + Physician Advisor
- **Actions**: Review denial reason, gather supporting documentation, draft appeal letter
- **Timeout**: Within payer appeal window (typically 30-60 days)

### Compliance Audit Trigger
- **Trigger**: Error rate >5% on random audit sample OR OIG Work Plan focus area
- **Reviewer**: Compliance Officer + HIM Director
- **Actions**: Expand audit scope, identify root cause, implement corrective education
- **Timeout**: 30 days for corrective action plan

name: Patient Intake & Registration
summary: Patient registration, insurance eligibility verification, and consent collection for ED and clinic visits.
description:
A patient intake and registration process handling 5,000-15,000 registrations per month across emergency department, ambulatory clinics, and scheduled admissions. The process captures demographics, verifies insurance eligibility in real-time, collects required consents and advance directives, and ensures accurate patient identity matching to prevent duplicate medical records.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Registration accuracy rate: >98%
- Insurance verification before service: >95%
- Average registration time: <5 minutes
- Duplicate record rate: <1%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **EMTALA** — medical screening cannot be delayed for registration/payment
- **HIPAA** — Notice of Privacy Practices provided at first encounter
- **Patient Self-Determination Act** — advance directive inquiry required at admission
- **Red Flags Rule** — identity theft prevention in patient identification

### Business Rules
- **Photo ID and insurance card** scanned at every visit
- Real-time **eligibility verification** (270/271 transaction) required before service
- **Consent for treatment** and **financial responsibility** signatures captured electronically
- Master Patient Index (MPI) search required — **no new record** created without duplicate check

### SLA & Performance
- ED registration: **<3 minutes** (bedside registration)
- Scheduled visit pre-registration: **24 hours** before appointment
- Insurance verification failures resolved within **1 business day**
human_approvals:
### VIP/Confidential Patient Handling
- **Trigger**: Patient requests confidential handling OR VIP flag activated
- **Reviewer**: Privacy Officer + Registration Supervisor
- **Actions**: Apply break-the-glass access controls, assign alias if needed, restrict record visibility
- **Timeout**: Immediate — before registration completes

### Uninsured/Financial Hardship Screening
- **Trigger**: No insurance verified OR self-pay patient
- **Reviewer**: Financial Counselor
- **Actions**: Screen for Medicaid eligibility, charity care, payment plans, presumptive eligibility
- **Timeout**: Before discharge for ED; at scheduling for elective

name: Hospital Bed Management
summary: Real-time bed assignment, patient flow optimization, and capacity forecasting across hospital units.
description:
A hospital bed management process optimizing patient placement across 200-800 beds spanning ICU, telemetry, medical-surgical, and observation units. The process coordinates admissions from ED, OR, direct admits, and transfers to minimize boarding times, balance census across units, and predict capacity constraints 12-24 hours ahead.

**Key performance metrics:**
- ED boarding time: <60 minutes
- Bed turnaround time: <45 minutes
- Occupancy rate target: 80-90%
- Patient placement accuracy (right bed, first time): >90%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **CMS Conditions of Participation** for patient rights (appropriate room assignment)
- **EMTALA** — patients cannot be held in ED for bed unavailability without ongoing care
- **Joint Commission** environment of care standards
- **State licensure** bed count limits and unit designations

### Business Rules
- **Isolation patients** must be placed in appropriate negative/positive pressure rooms
- **Gender-appropriate** room assignments mandatory for semi-private rooms
- **Discharge orders** before noon flagged for priority environmental services turnover
- **Bed requests** processed FIFO with clinical acuity override

### SLA & Performance
- Bed assigned within **30 minutes** of admission order
- Bed cleaned and ready within **45 minutes** of discharge
- Transfer patients accepted within **2 hours** of approval
human_approvals:
### Capacity Escalation (Code Yellow/Red)
- **Trigger**: Occupancy >92% OR ED boarders >10 OR predicted shortfall in 12 hours
- **Reviewer**: Bed Management Director + CNO
- **Actions**: Activate surge protocols, expedite discharges, open overflow areas, divert ambulances if needed
- **Timeout**: 2 hours → escalate to Hospital Incident Command

### ICU Triage Priority
- **Trigger**: Multiple ICU admission requests exceeding available beds
- **Reviewer**: ICU Attending + Critical Care Triage Officer
- **Actions**: Apply triage criteria, assess step-down candidates, coordinate transfer to partner facility
- **Timeout**: 30 minutes — life-safety priority

name: Clinical Documentation Improvement
summary: CDI query process for accurate clinical documentation, coding specificity, and DRG optimization.
description:
A clinical documentation improvement program reviewing 2,000-5,000 inpatient encounters per month to ensure physician documentation accurately reflects patient acuity, severity of illness, and risk of mortality. CDI specialists identify documentation gaps, issue clarification queries to physicians, and collaborate with coding to ensure accurate DRG assignment and quality reporting.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Query response rate: >85%
- Query agreement rate: >75%
- Case mix index (CMI) improvement: +5-10%
- CC/MCC capture rate: >60%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **AHIMA/ACDIS Query Practice Brief** compliant query standards (non-leading, clinically relevant)
- **CMS ICD-10 Official Guidelines** for code assignment
- **OIG** scrutiny on DRG accuracy and upcoding
- **Quality measure reporting** (PSI, HAC, Leapfrog) impacted by documentation

### Business Rules
- Queries must be **clinically validated** and never leading or assumptive
- All queries require **physician attestation** — no code assigned without documentation support
- **Concurrent review** must begin within **24 hours** of admission
- Working DRG reviewed at **discharge** for reconciliation with final coding

### SLA & Performance
- CDI review initiated within **24 hours** of admission
- Physician query response within **48 hours**
- Post-discharge reconciliation within **5 business days**
human_approvals:
### Physician Query Escalation
- **Trigger**: Query unanswered after 48 hours OR query disagreement by physician
- **Reviewer**: CDI Manager + Physician Champion
- **Actions**: Re-engage physician, provide clinical rationale, escalate to department chair if needed
- **Timeout**: 72 hours → escalate to CMO/VPMA

### Compliance Review of Query Patterns
- **Trigger**: Individual query agreement rate <50% OR leading/non-compliant query identified
- **Reviewer**: Compliance Officer + CDI Director
- **Actions**: Audit query templates, provide re-education, implement corrective action
- **Timeout**: 30 days for corrective action plan

name: Radiology Order Fulfillment
summary: Imaging order triage, scheduling, protocol assignment, reading, and results delivery.
description:
A radiology order fulfillment process managing 3,000-10,000 imaging studies per month across modalities (CT, MRI, X-ray, ultrasound, interventional). The process covers order validation, clinical decision support checks, scheduling, protocol assignment, image acquisition, radiologist reading, and structured reporting with critical results notification.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Report turnaround (routine): <24 hours
- Report turnaround (STAT): <1 hour
- Critical result communication: <15 minutes
- Appropriate use criteria compliance: >90%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ACR Appropriateness Criteria** for clinical decision support
- **Joint Commission NPSG** critical result reporting requirements
- **MQSA** for mammography quality standards
- **Radiation dose tracking** and ALARA principles

### Business Rules
- All orders must pass **CDS appropriateness check** (AUC mandate for advanced imaging)
- **STAT studies** prioritized in reading queue — radiologist acknowledgment within **15 minutes**
- **Prior images** automatically loaded for comparison when available
- **Addendum** rate tracked — >5% triggers quality review

### SLA & Performance
- STAT report: **<1 hour** from image availability
- Routine report: **<24 hours**
- Scheduled exams: patient wait time **<15 minutes** from appointment time
human_approvals:
### Critical/Unexpected Finding
- **Trigger**: Critical result identified (e.g., PE, stroke, pneumothorax, aortic dissection)
- **Reviewer**: Radiologist → direct communication to ordering physician
- **Actions**: Immediate verbal notification, document communication, escalate if ordering physician unreachable
- **Timeout**: 15 minutes → escalate to charge nurse/ED attending

### Contrast Allergy/Renal Risk Review
- **Trigger**: GFR <30 OR documented contrast allergy OR pregnancy
- **Reviewer**: Radiologist + Ordering Physician
- **Actions**: Evaluate risk/benefit, consider alternative study, pre-medicate if proceeding
- **Timeout**: 2 hours — study held until review complete

name: Batch Release Processing
summary: GMP batch record review, QA release decision, and certificate of analysis issuance for drug products.
description:
A pharmaceutical batch release process managing 50-200 batch dispositions per month across oral solid dose, injectable, and topical product lines. The process encompasses batch record review, in-process and finished product testing, deviation assessment, stability data review, and Qualified Person (QP) certification before market release.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Right-first-time batch record closure: >90%
- Average batch release cycle: <10 business days
- Rejected batches: <2%
- Regulatory observation rate: <0.5 per audit
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **21 CFR 211** (US cGMP) and **EU GMP Annex 16** batch certification
- **ICH Q7** for API manufacturing requirements
- **EU Falsified Medicines Directive** serialization verification before release
- **Data integrity** per MHRA/FDA guidance (ALCOA+ principles)

### Business Rules
- **No batch released** with open critical or major deviations
- All raw material **CoAs verified** against approved specifications before use
- **Environmental monitoring** data reviewed as part of batch release for sterile products
- **Stability indicating** test methods required for all release testing
- **Annual Product Review (APR)** data updated with each batch release

### SLA & Performance
- Batch record review completed within **5 business days** of manufacturing
- QC testing completed within **7 business days** of sample receipt
- QP release within **2 business days** of complete dossier
human_approvals:
### Batch Deviation Impact Assessment
- **Trigger**: Deviation recorded during batch manufacturing OR OOS test result
- **Reviewer**: QA Manager + Production Manager
- **Actions**: Classify deviation (critical/major/minor), assess product impact, determine batch disposition
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for critical deviations → escalate to Site Quality Director

### Qualified Person (QP) Release
- **Trigger**: All batch record review complete, testing passed, deviations closed
- **Reviewer**: Qualified Person (EU) or Authorized Person
- **Actions**: Verify GMP compliance, confirm specifications met, certify batch for distribution
- **Timeout**: 48 hours → escalate to Head of Quality

### Out-of-Specification (OOS) Investigation
- **Trigger**: Any test result outside registered specification
- **Reviewer**: QC Manager + QA Investigation Team
- **Actions**: Phase I lab investigation (24h), Phase II manufacturing investigation if confirmed, CAPA
- **Timeout**: 30 days for investigation closure per FDA guidance

name: Pharmacovigilance Case Processing
summary: Individual case safety report (ICSR) intake, medical assessment, and regulatory submission worldwide.
description:
A pharmacovigilance case processing workflow handling 500-5,000 individual case safety reports (ICSRs) per month from spontaneous reports, clinical trials, literature, and solicited programs. The process covers case intake, data entry, medical assessment, causality evaluation, narrative authoring, and expedited/periodic regulatory reporting to FDA, EMA, and 100+ health authorities worldwide.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Expedited report submission compliance: 100%
- Average case processing time: <10 calendar days
- Data quality score: >95%
- Regulatory submission on-time rate: >99%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ICH E2B(R3)** electronic ICSR reporting format
- **FDA 21 CFR 314.80/600.80** post-marketing safety reporting (15-day expedited, periodic)
- **EU GVP Module VI** management of safety reports
- **EudraVigilance** and **FAERS** database submissions

### Business Rules
- All **serious unexpected** cases reported to regulators within **15 calendar days**
- **Fatal/life-threatening** cases reported within **7 calendar days** (EU) or **15 days** (FDA)
- All cases entered into safety database within **3 calendar days** of receipt (Day 0 = awareness)
- **Follow-up** attempted for all serious cases missing key information
- **Literature surveillance** conducted weekly for marketed products

### SLA & Performance
- Day 0 determination within **24 hours** of initial receipt
- Case data entry within **3 calendar days**
- Medical assessment within **7 calendar days**
- Expedited report submission on Day **15** (100% compliance)
human_approvals:
### Serious/Unexpected Case Triage
- **Trigger**: Case classified as serious AND unexpected (not in label/SmPC)
- **Reviewer**: Drug Safety Physician
- **Actions**: Medical assessment, causality determination, signal evaluation, expedited reporting decision
- **Timeout**: 24 hours of initial receipt — regulatory clock starts at Day 0

### Signal Detection Review
- **Trigger**: Statistical signal detected (disproportionality analysis) OR case series pattern identified
- **Reviewer**: Signal Management Team (Safety Physician, Epidemiologist, Medical Affairs)
- **Actions**: Validate signal, assess clinical significance, determine if label update or DHPC needed
- **Timeout**: 30 days for signal validation → PRAC reporting if confirmed

### Benefit-Risk Assessment Trigger
- **Trigger**: Cumulative safety data suggests unfavorable benefit-risk OR regulatory authority request
- **Reviewer**: Safety Governance Board + Chief Medical Officer
- **Actions**: Comprehensive benefit-risk evaluation, risk minimization measures, regulatory communication
- **Timeout**: Per regulatory authority timeline (typically 30-60 days)

name: Regulatory Submission Management
summary: NDA/BLA/MAA dossier preparation, eCTD compilation, and regulatory authority submission management.
description:
A regulatory submission management process coordinating the preparation and filing of marketing authorization applications (NDA, BLA, MAA, ANDA) and post-approval supplements across FDA, EMA, and 50+ health authorities. The process manages eCTD document lifecycle, cross-functional content authoring, publishing, and submission tracking for 20-100 regulatory actions per year.

**Key performance metrics:**
- On-time submission rate: >98%
- First-cycle approval rate: >70%
- Deficiency response turnaround: <30 days
- eCTD validation error rate: <1%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ICH CTD/eCTD** format requirements (M1-M5 module structure)
- **FDA 21 CFR 314/601** NDA/BLA requirements
- **EU Regulation 726/2004** centralized procedure requirements
- **ICH M4** granularity and organization of CTD modules

### Business Rules
- All submissions must pass **eCTD technical validation** before filing
- **Regulatory strategy document** approved before submission initiation
- Cross-reference to **DMFs and CEPs** must be current and verified
- **Commitments tracker** updated within **5 days** of health authority interaction

### SLA & Performance
- Submission published and filed on **target date** (98% on-time)
- Information request responses within **regulatory clock-stop** deadlines
- Annual report submissions within **60 days** of anniversary date
human_approvals:
### Submission Content Review
- **Trigger**: Module content draft completed (CMC, clinical, nonclinical)
- **Reviewer**: Regulatory Strategy Lead + Subject Matter Expert
- **Actions**: Review for regulatory compliance, verify data consistency, approve for publishing
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to VP Regulatory

### Health Authority Deficiency Response
- **Trigger**: Information Request (IR), Refuse-to-File, or Complete Response Letter received
- **Reviewer**: Regulatory Lead + Cross-functional Response Team
- **Actions**: Assess deficiency scope, assign action items, prepare response strategy, coordinate with health authority
- **Timeout**: Per regulatory timeline (FDA CRL: 12 months, EMA Day 120 clock-stop varies)

name: Clinical Trial Supply Management
summary: Clinical trial material forecasting, packaging, labeling, depot management, and investigational site supply.
description:
A clinical supply chain process managing investigational medicinal product (IMP) supply for 10-50 active clinical trials across 200+ investigator sites globally. The process covers demand forecasting based on enrollment projections, GMP packaging and labeling, IRT-driven randomization and supply allocation, depot network management, and returns/destruction.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Site stock-out rate: <1%
- Supply waste (expiry/destruction): <10%
- Packaging-to-shipment cycle: <5 business days
- IRT order fulfillment: >99% on-time
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **EU GMP Annex 13** investigational medicinal product requirements
- **21 CFR 211.137** expiry dating and stability-based shelf life
- **ICH GCP E6(R2)** investigational product accountability
- **Country-specific import** licenses and clinical trial authorizations

### Business Rules
- All IMP must be **QP released** before shipment to depot/site
- **Temperature monitoring** required for all shipments (2-8°C, -20°C, -80°C as applicable)
- **Blinding integrity** maintained throughout packaging, labeling, and distribution
- Interactive Response Technology (IRT) controls all **randomization and dispensing**

### SLA & Performance
- Depot-to-site shipment within **3 business days** of IRT order
- Packaging turnaround: **10 business days** from bulk receipt
- Monthly supply status reports to **Sponsor** and **DMC** if applicable
human_approvals:
### Supply Shortage Escalation
- **Trigger**: Site inventory below safety stock OR supply forecast shows shortfall within 8 weeks
- **Reviewer**: Clinical Supply Manager + Study Director
- **Actions**: Reallocate depot inventory, expedite packaging run, implement enrollment hold if critical
- **Timeout**: 48 hours → escalate to Head of Clinical Operations

### Label/Packaging Deviation
- **Trigger**: Packaging or labeling error discovered during QA inspection
- **Reviewer**: QA Manager + Clinical Supply Lead
- **Actions**: Quarantine affected units, assess patient safety impact, determine rework or destruction
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for assessment → regulatory notification if distributed

name: Manufacturing Deviation Management
summary: GMP deviation investigation, root cause analysis, and CAPA implementation in pharmaceutical manufacturing.
description:
A pharmaceutical deviation management process handling 100-500 deviations per month across manufacturing, packaging, laboratory, and warehouse operations. The process covers deviation identification, classification (critical/major/minor), root cause investigation (Ishikawa, 5-Why, fault tree), impact assessment, CAPA implementation, and effectiveness verification.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Investigation closure within target: >85%
- Recurring deviation rate: <10%
- CAPA effectiveness (no recurrence in 12 months): >90%
- Regulatory observation rate: <1 per audit related to deviations
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **21 CFR 211.192** investigation of discrepancies (US FDA)
- **EU GMP Chapter 1.4** deviation management requirements
- **ICH Q10** pharmaceutical quality system — CAPA elements
- **Data integrity** requirements for investigation records

### Business Rules
- All deviations **documented within 24 hours** of discovery
- Critical deviations require **management notification within 4 hours**
- Root cause investigation completed within **30 days** (critical/major) or **60 days** (minor)
- **CAPA effectiveness** verified 6-12 months post-implementation
- **Trending** reviewed monthly at Quality Council

### SLA & Performance
- Deviation logged within **24 hours** of occurrence
- Critical investigation: **30 days** closure target
- CAPA implementation within **90 days** of approval
human_approvals:
### Critical Deviation Triage
- **Trigger**: Deviation classified as critical (product quality/patient safety impact)
- **Reviewer**: Site Quality Director + Production Director
- **Actions**: Immediate containment, batch hold decision, preliminary root cause within 24h, regulatory notification assessment
- **Timeout**: 4 hours for containment → 24 hours for initial assessment

### CAPA Approval
- **Trigger**: Root cause confirmed and corrective/preventive actions proposed
- **Reviewer**: QA Manager + Process Owner + Validation Lead
- **Actions**: Assess CAPA adequacy, approve implementation plan, assign effectiveness check date
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → escalate to Quality Council

name: GMP Audit Management
summary: Internal and external GMP audit planning, execution, findings management, and regulatory inspection readiness.
description:
A GMP audit management process coordinating 50-150 audits per year including internal self-inspections, supplier/CMO audits, and regulatory inspection hosting (FDA, EMA, MHRA, TGA). The process covers risk-based audit scheduling, audit team qualification, protocol preparation, on-site execution, finding classification, response management, and CAPA tracking.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Audit schedule adherence: >95%
- Critical findings per audit: <0.5 average
- CAPA closure on-time: >90%
- Regulatory inspection readiness score: >90%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **EU GMP Chapter 9** self-inspection requirements (annual minimum)
- **21 CFR 211** GMP requirements subject to FDA inspection
- **ICH Q10** monitoring system and management review
- **Supplier qualification** per EU GMP Chapter 5 and 21 CFR 211.84

### Business Rules
- **Risk-based audit schedule** updated annually using quality risk management principles
- All auditors must meet **qualification requirements** (training, experience, independence)
- **Supplier audits** required before approval and at defined frequency based on risk
- **Mock inspections** conducted annually for each manufacturing site

### SLA & Performance
- Audit report issued within **15 business days** of audit completion
- CAPA responses within **30 days** (internal) or per auditee agreement
- Regulatory inspection responses within **regulatory timeline** (FDA: 15 business days)
human_approvals:
### Critical Audit Finding
- **Trigger**: Finding classified as critical (data integrity, patient safety, systemic failure)
- **Reviewer**: Site Quality Director + Global Quality VP
- **Actions**: Immediate corrective action, root cause investigation, regulatory notification assessment, executive briefing
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for immediate action plan → 15 business days for formal response

### Regulatory Inspection Management
- **Trigger**: Regulatory authority announces inspection (FDA 483, EMA GMP inspection)
- **Reviewer**: Inspection Readiness Team (QA, Production, QC, Regulatory)
- **Actions**: Activate inspection SOP, assign back-room team, coordinate document requests, daily debrief
- **Timeout**: Inspection dates are non-negotiable — immediate mobilization

name: Stability Study Management
summary: ICH stability study design, execution, trending, and shelf-life determination for drug products.
description:
A pharmaceutical stability study management process handling 100-300 active stability protocols across commercial products, new submissions, and post-approval commitments. The process covers study design per ICH guidelines (Q1A-Q1E), sample pull scheduling, testing coordination, trending analysis, out-of-trend investigations, and shelf-life/retest period recommendations.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Sample pull on-time rate: >98%
- Testing completion within protocol window: >95%
- OOT investigation closure: <30 days
- Regulatory commitment compliance: 100%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ICH Q1A(R2)** stability testing for new drug substances/products
- **ICH Q1E** evaluation of stability data for shelf-life determination
- **ICH Q5C** stability testing of biotechnological/biological products
- **21 CFR 211.166** stability testing requirements

### Business Rules
- All commercial products on **ongoing stability** program (at least 1 batch/year/strength)
- **Post-approval commitments** tracked and reported per regulatory agreements
- Storage conditions per ICH: **25°C/60%RH** (long-term), **40°C/75%RH** (accelerated)
- **Out-of-specification** results on stability trigger batch disposition review

### SLA & Performance
- Sample pulls completed within **±2 days** of scheduled timepoint
- Testing completed within **30 days** of sample pull
- Annual stability reports issued within **Q1** of following year
human_approvals:
### Out-of-Trend (OOT) Investigation
- **Trigger**: Stability result shows statistically significant trend toward specification limit
- **Reviewer**: Stability Scientist + QA Manager
- **Actions**: Confirm trend, investigate root cause, assess shelf-life impact, determine if specification change needed
- **Timeout**: 30 days for investigation → notify Regulatory if shelf-life impacted

### Shelf-Life Extension/Reduction Decision
- **Trigger**: Sufficient long-term data supports change OR adverse trend confirmed
- **Reviewer**: Stability Director + Regulatory Affairs
- **Actions**: Statistical analysis, regulatory strategy, supplement/variation filing decision
- **Timeout**: 60 days for data package preparation

name: Serialization & Track-and-Trace
summary: Product serialization, aggregation hierarchy, and regulatory track-and-trace compliance (DSCSA, EU FMD).
description:
A pharmaceutical serialization process managing unique identifier assignment, printing, verification, aggregation, and regulatory reporting for all saleable drug product units. The process supports compliance with US DSCSA (Drug Supply Chain Security Act), EU Falsified Medicines Directive, and 40+ country-specific serialization mandates, handling 10-50 million serial numbers annually.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Serialization success rate (print & verify): >99.5%
- Aggregation accuracy: >99.9%
- Regulatory upload success rate: 100%
- Exception management resolution: <48 hours
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **DSCSA** (US) — full interoperable track-and-trace by Nov 2024+
- **EU FMD (2011/62/EU)** — unique identifier verification at point of dispense
- **GS1 GTIN/SGTIN** standards for product identification
- **Country-specific** mandates (Brazil ANVISA, China NMPA, Russia MDLP, etc.)

### Business Rules
- Every saleable unit receives **unique serial number** in GS1 SGTIN format
- **Aggregation hierarchy**: unit → bundle → case → pallet tracked in L4 repository
- **Commissioned serial numbers** cannot be reused — decommission and recommission protocol required
- **Verification** at point of dispense within mandated countries

### SLA & Performance
- Serial number provisioning within **24 hours** of packaging line request
- Regulatory upload within **24 hours** of shipment
- Exception resolution within **48 hours**
human_approvals:
### Serialization Line Exception
- **Trigger**: Rejection rate >2% on packaging line OR aggregation mismatch detected
- **Reviewer**: Packaging Supervisor + Serialization Engineer
- **Actions**: Halt line, diagnose issue (camera, printer, data), rework or reserialize affected units
- **Timeout**: 2 hours → escalate to Engineering Manager

### Regulatory Data Submission Failure
- **Trigger**: Upload to NMVS/EMVS/FDA DSCSA system fails OR data rejected by repository
- **Reviewer**: Serialization Manager + IT/Integration Team
- **Actions**: Diagnose rejection reason, correct data, resubmit, verify acceptance
- **Timeout**: 24 hours → shipment hold until resolved

name: Student Enrollment & Registration
summary: Student admissions processing, enrollment confirmation, course registration, and academic onboarding.
description:
A higher education enrollment process managing 5,000-20,000 applications per admissions cycle across undergraduate, graduate, and professional programs. The process spans application review, admissions decision, enrollment deposit, orientation, course registration, and academic record creation with integration to SIS, LMS, and financial systems.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Application-to-enrollment yield: >30%
- Average decision turnaround: <21 days
- Registration completion rate: >95%
- First-year retention rate: >85%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FERPA** compliance for all student educational records
- **Title IX** non-discrimination in admissions
- **ADA/Section 504** accommodations in admissions and registration
- **State authorization** for distance education programs

### Business Rules
- **Enrollment deposit** required within **30 days** of acceptance to hold seat
- Students must complete **orientation** before course registration enabled
- **Academic holds** (financial, immunization, advising) block registration until cleared
- **Waitlist** managed automatically — students have **48 hours** to accept opened seat

### SLA & Performance
- Admissions decisions within **21 days** of complete application
- Transfer credit evaluation within **10 business days**
- Registration period opens **90 days** before term start
human_approvals:
### Admissions Committee Review
- **Trigger**: Applicant GPA below threshold OR holistic review required (test-optional, special talent)
- **Reviewer**: Admissions Committee (faculty + admissions officers)
- **Actions**: Holistic file review, interview scheduling (if applicable), scholarship consideration, admit/deny/waitlist
- **Timeout**: 10 business days → escalate to Dean of Admissions

### Enrollment Exception/Override
- **Trigger**: Student requests course overload, prerequisite waiver, or closed section override
- **Reviewer**: Academic Advisor + Department Chair
- **Actions**: Verify academic standing, assess appropriateness, approve/deny with conditions
- **Timeout**: 48 hours during registration period

name: Financial Aid Processing
summary: FAFSA verification, aid packaging, disbursement, and federal Title IV compliance.
description:
A financial aid processing workflow managing 10,000-30,000 aid applications per award year across federal (Pell, Direct Loans, SEOG), state, institutional, and private scholarship programs. The process covers FAFSA receipt, verification selection, need analysis, aid packaging, award notification, disbursement scheduling, and Return of Title IV (R2T4) calculations for withdrawn students.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Aid packaging turnaround: <10 business days from verification complete
- FAFSA verification completion rate: >95%
- Disbursement accuracy: >99.5%
- Default rate: <10% (3-year CDR)

**Annual cycle:** October FAFSA filing → spring packaging → summer disbursement → fall enrollment census
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Title IV HEA** compliance (34 CFR Parts 668, 682, 685, 690)
- **FAFSA Simplification Act** updated need analysis methodology
- **Clery Act** disclosures and campus safety reporting
- **Gainful Employment** program eligibility requirements
- **Annual compliance audit** (A-133/Uniform Guidance)

### Business Rules
- **Verification** completed before any Title IV funds disbursed
- **Enrollment census** on Day 15 — aid adjusted for drops/adds
- Federal loans require **entrance counseling** and signed **MPN** before disbursement
- **R2T4 calculation** completed within **30 days** of withdrawal date determination
- **Institutional scholarships** require minimum **2.5 GPA** maintained each semester

### SLA & Performance
- Award letters sent within **10 business days** of completed file
- Disbursement within **3 business days** of enrollment verification
- R2T4 returns to ED within **45 days** of withdrawal
human_approvals:
### Professional Judgment Appeal
- **Trigger**: Student/family requests income adjustment due to special circumstances (job loss, medical, divorce)
- **Reviewer**: Financial Aid Officer (must hold PJ authority)
- **Actions**: Collect supporting documentation, verify circumstances, recalculate EFC, adjust aid package
- **Timeout**: 15 business days → escalate to Director of Financial Aid

### Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Appeal
- **Trigger**: Student fails to meet SAP standards (GPA, completion rate, maximum timeframe)
- **Reviewer**: SAP Appeals Committee
- **Actions**: Review academic plan, assess extenuating circumstances, approve/deny continued eligibility
- **Timeout**: 10 business days before next disbursement date

### Over-Award/Over-Payment Resolution
- **Trigger**: Student receives aid exceeding cost of attendance OR enrollment status changes
- **Reviewer**: Financial Aid Accountant + Compliance Officer
- **Actions**: Calculate adjustment, reduce institutional aid first, return Title IV funds per R2T4 formula
- **Timeout**: Within 45 days per federal requirement

name: Accreditation & Compliance Review
summary: Institutional accreditation self-study, peer review, and continuous improvement reporting.
description:
An accreditation compliance process managing regional and programmatic accreditation cycles (HLC, SACSCOC, AACSB, ABET, etc.) including self-study preparation, evidence collection, peer review coordination, and ongoing compliance reporting. The process supports 5-15 accreditation relationships with varying review cycles (5-10 years) and annual reporting requirements.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Accreditation maintenance rate: 100%
- Self-study completion: 6 months before site visit
- Assessment data collection compliance: >90%
- Peer review team satisfaction: >4.0/5.0
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **HEA Title IV** — accreditation required for federal financial aid eligibility
- **USDE recognition** of accrediting agencies
- **State authorization** alignment with accreditation standards
- Discipline-specific standards (AACSB, ABET, CCNE, ABA, etc.)

### Business Rules
- **Annual assessment reports** submitted by all academic programs by June 30
- **Student learning outcome** data collected and analyzed every **academic year**
- Self-study process begins **18 months** before scheduled site visit
- **Substantive change** notifications filed **before** implementation

### SLA & Performance
- Accreditation reports submitted on **accreditor deadline** (100%)
- Assessment data collected within **60 days** of term end
- Improvement action plans implemented within **one academic year**
human_approvals:
### Accreditation Risk Alert
- **Trigger**: Program outcome data below threshold OR compliance issue identified
- **Reviewer**: Provost + Assessment Director + Program Dean
- **Actions**: Analyze root cause, develop improvement plan, prepare interim report to accreditor
- **Timeout**: 30 days for improvement plan → board notification if accreditation at risk

### Self-Study Chapter Approval
- **Trigger**: Chapter draft completed by writing team
- **Reviewer**: Self-Study Steering Committee + External Reviewer
- **Actions**: Review evidence adequacy, verify claims supported by data, approve or return for revision
- **Timeout**: 15 business days per chapter review cycle

name: Student Conduct Adjudication
summary: Student misconduct investigation, hearing process, and sanctions with due process protections.
description:
A student conduct adjudication process managing 200-1,000 cases per academic year across academic integrity violations, code of conduct breaches, and Title IX-related matters. The process ensures fair and consistent adjudication through investigation, notice, hearing/resolution, sanctioning, and appeal procedures consistent with constitutional due process requirements.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Case resolution within target timeline: >90%
- Appeal rate: <15%
- Sanction compliance rate: >95%
- Respondent/complainant satisfaction with process fairness: >75%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Title IX** (34 CFR Part 106) sexual harassment grievance procedures
- **Due Process** (5th/14th Amendment for public institutions)
- **Clery Act** timely warning and crime statistics reporting
- **FERPA** protections for conduct records

### Business Rules
- **Written notice** of charges provided at least **5 business days** before hearing
- Respondent has right to **advisor of choice** (including attorney for Title IX)
- **Standard of proof**: preponderance of evidence (>50%)
- **Academic integrity** first offense: educational sanction; second offense: conduct charge
- All conduct records retained for **7 years** after graduation/separation

### SLA & Performance
- Initial notice within **3 business days** of report/complaint
- Investigation completed within **60 business days**
- Appeal decisions within **10 business days** of appeal submission
human_approvals:
### Title IX Formal Complaint
- **Trigger**: Formal complaint of sexual harassment or sexual violence filed
- **Reviewer**: Title IX Coordinator + Trained Investigator
- **Actions**: Issue notice, supportive measures, appoint investigator, live hearing with cross-examination
- **Timeout**: 90 days for investigation → hearing within 30 days of report

### Suspension/Expulsion Recommendation
- **Trigger**: Hearing panel recommends suspension >1 semester or expulsion
- **Reviewer**: VP of Student Affairs + Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Review hearing record, verify due process, approve/modify sanction
- **Timeout**: 5 business days → final decision communicated to student

name: Curriculum Approval & Governance
summary: New program and course proposal review through faculty governance committees and state/board approval.
description:
A curriculum governance process managing 100-300 proposals per academic year for new programs, course modifications, and program closures. The process flows through department, college, and university-level curriculum committees, faculty senate, and external approvals (board of trustees, state higher education board, accreditors) with assessment planning embedded at each stage.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Proposal approval cycle: <120 days (internal)
- Faculty governance participation rate: >80%
- External approval success rate: >95%
- New program launch on-schedule: >90%
business_constraints:
