### Regulatory Requirements
- **State Higher Education Board** approval for new degree programs
- **USDE** notification for new programs eligible for Title IV aid
- **Accreditor** substantive change notification/approval
- **Board of Trustees** authorization for new degrees

### Business Rules
- All proposals must include **student learning outcomes** aligned to institutional goals
- **Assessment plan** required before program launch approval
- Course changes affecting **general education** require university curriculum committee review
- **Resource impact statement** required for all new programs (faculty, space, technology, library)

### SLA & Performance
- Department-level review within **15 business days**
- College committee review within **30 business days**
- University governance cycle: monthly meetings September-May
human_approvals:
### Program Viability Assessment
- **Trigger**: New degree program proposal submitted
- **Reviewer**: Provost Office + Institutional Research + Budget Committee
- **Actions**: Market demand analysis, enrollment projections, resource requirements, financial pro forma review
- **Timeout**: 30 days → return to proposer with questions or approval to proceed

### External Approval Submission
- **Trigger**: Internal governance approval complete for new program
- **Reviewer**: Provost + Regulatory Compliance Office
- **Actions**: Prepare state board application, accreditor notification, USDE program addition (if Title IV)
- **Timeout**: Per external body timelines (typically 3-12 months)

name: Production Scheduling & Optimization
summary: MRP/APS-driven production scheduling with capacity planning, constraint management, and S&OP alignment.
description:
A manufacturing production scheduling process managing 500-2,000 work orders per week across multiple production lines, work centers, and shifts. The process integrates material requirements planning (MRP), advanced planning and scheduling (APS), and finite capacity modeling to optimize throughput, minimize changeovers, and meet customer delivery commitments.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Schedule adherence: >92%
- On-time delivery (OTIF): >95%
- Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE): >80%
- Changeover time reduction: 15% year-over-year
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **OSHA** workplace safety for shift scheduling and overtime limits
- **EPA** production emissions permits and batch limits
- **ISO 9001** quality management in production planning
- **Customer-specific** scheduling requirements (automotive MMOG/LE)

### Business Rules
- **Frozen schedule** window: 48 hours — no changes without VP approval
- **Safety stock** maintained at 2 weeks supply for A-class items
- **Changeover optimization** — group like products to minimize setup time
- Material availability confirmed **72 hours** before scheduled start

### SLA & Performance
- Schedule published **weekly** on Thursday for following week
- Rush order response within **4 hours**
- Capacity utilization reports **daily** by 7 AM
human_approvals:
### Rush Order Insertion
- **Trigger**: Customer priority order with lead time < standard planning fence
- **Reviewer**: Production Planner + Sales Manager
- **Actions**: Assess capacity impact, evaluate displacement of existing orders, approve/reject with revised promise date
- **Timeout**: 4 hours → escalate to VP Operations

### Capacity Overload Resolution
- **Trigger**: Work center utilization >110% in planning period
- **Reviewer**: Production Manager + Maintenance Lead
- **Actions**: Authorize overtime, activate alternate work center, outsource, or reschedule lower-priority orders
- **Timeout**: 24 hours → escalate to Plant Director

name: Incoming Quality Inspection
summary: Raw material and component receiving inspection with supplier scorecards and corrective action management.
description:
A manufacturing incoming quality inspection process handling 1,000-5,000 material receipts per month with sampling plans based on supplier performance tiers. The process covers receiving inspection, dimensional and functional testing, supplier corrective action requests (SCARs), and material disposition decisions.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Incoming defect rate: <500 PPM
- Inspection turnaround: <24 hours (standard), <4 hours (line-down)
- Supplier on-time quality: >98%
- SCAR closure rate: >90% within 30 days
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ISO 9001:2015** clause 8.4 — control of externally provided processes
- **IATF 16949** (automotive) — incoming product verification requirements
- **AS9100** (aerospace) — first article inspection and material traceability
- **RoHS/REACH** material compliance verification

### Business Rules
- **Skip-lot inspection** earned after 10 consecutive lots accepted from Tier 1 suppliers
- **Critical characteristics** require **100% inspection** regardless of supplier tier
- **First article inspection** (FAI) required for all new parts and after process changes
- Non-conforming material held in **quarantine** until disposition — no production use

### SLA & Performance
- Standard inspection within **24 hours** of receipt
- Line-down materials within **4 hours**
- SCAR issued within **48 hours** of rejection
human_approvals:
### Material Rejection & Disposition
- **Trigger**: Inspection failure — material does not meet specification
- **Reviewer**: Quality Engineer + Procurement Lead
- **Actions**: Quarantine material, issue SCAR to supplier, evaluate use-as-is/rework/return/scrap options
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for disposition decision → 48 hours for supplier notification

### Use-As-Is Concession
- **Trigger**: Non-conforming material needed for production, deviation within acceptable risk
- **Reviewer**: Quality Manager + Design Engineering
- **Actions**: Engineering evaluation, risk assessment, documented concession with lot traceability
- **Timeout**: 8 hours for line-down situations

name: Equipment Maintenance Management
summary: Preventive and predictive maintenance scheduling, work order management, and spare parts optimization.
description:
A manufacturing maintenance management process handling 500-2,000 work orders per month across production equipment, utilities, and facility systems. The process covers preventive maintenance scheduling, predictive analytics (vibration, thermal, oil analysis), breakdown response, spare parts management, and reliability improvement through RCA and FMEA.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Planned vs. unplanned maintenance ratio: >80:20
- Mean time between failure (MTBF): increasing trend
- Mean time to repair (MTTR): <4 hours
- PM completion rate: >95%
- Spare parts availability: >98%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **OSHA** lockout/tagout (LOTO) compliance for all maintenance activities
- **EPA** leak detection and repair (LDAR) for regulated equipment
- **Insurance carrier** requirements for critical equipment inspection
- **Pressure vessel** and **boiler** inspection per ASME/state regulations

### Business Rules
- **PM schedules** based on OEM recommendations and reliability data (not just calendar)
- All maintenance **requires work order** — no undocumented repairs
- **Safety permits** (hot work, confined space, line break) completed before work starts
- **Spare parts** for critical equipment maintained at minimum **2 units** safety stock

### SLA & Performance
- Emergency response: technician on-site within **15 minutes**
- PM work orders completed within **scheduled window** (95% target)
- Completed work orders closed within **24 hours** of completion
human_approvals:
### Emergency Breakdown Response
- **Trigger**: Unplanned equipment failure causing production line stoppage
- **Reviewer**: Maintenance Supervisor + Production Manager
- **Actions**: Dispatch technician, assess repair scope, activate backup equipment, estimate downtime
- **Timeout**: 15 minutes for response, 2 hours for repair plan → escalate to Maintenance Manager

### Capital Repair/Replacement Decision
- **Trigger**: Repair cost >50% of replacement value OR repeated failures (>3 in 90 days)
- **Reviewer**: Maintenance Manager + Finance + Plant Director
- **Actions**: Cost-benefit analysis, evaluate lease/buy options, prepare capital request
- **Timeout**: 5 business days for analysis

name: Shop Floor Execution & Tracking
summary: MES-driven work order execution with operator instructions, real-time tracking, and full lot traceability.
description:
A shop floor execution process managing real-time production tracking across 10-50 work centers, capturing labor, material consumption, quality data, and equipment status. The process provides digital work instructions, enforces process routing, and ensures complete lot/serial traceability from raw material to finished goods.

**Key performance metrics:**
- First-pass yield: >97%
- Production reporting accuracy: >99%
- Cycle time variance: <5%
- Traceability completeness: 100%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FDA 21 CFR Part 820** (medical devices) — device history record requirements
- **ISO 13485** production and traceability for regulated products
- **IATF 16949** automotive traceability and control plan adherence
- **ALCOA+ data integrity** for electronic batch/production records

### Business Rules
- **No production** without released work order and approved routing
- **Material consumption** recorded at each operation step (backflush or manual)
- **Operator qualification** verified by MES before allowing operation execution
- **Lot/serial genealogy** maintained — forward and backward traceability within 4 hours

### SLA & Performance
- Real-time production status visible to planning within **5 minutes** of operation completion
- Quality data entry at point of **production** (no end-of-shift batching)
- Shift production reports available within **30 minutes** of shift end
human_approvals:
### Quality Hold at Process Step
- **Trigger**: In-process measurement out of specification OR SPC control limit breach
- **Reviewer**: Quality Technician + Line Supervisor
- **Actions**: Stop production at station, quarantine suspect units, investigate root cause, determine disposition
- **Timeout**: 30 minutes → escalate to Quality Engineer

### Process Deviation Authorization
- **Trigger**: Operator requests to deviate from standard routing or work instruction
- **Reviewer**: Manufacturing Engineer + Quality
- **Actions**: Evaluate technical risk, document temporary deviation, set expiry/review date
- **Timeout**: 2 hours for production-critical deviations

name: Demand Planning & Forecasting
summary: Statistical demand forecasting, consensus planning, and S&OP process alignment across sales and operations.
description:
A demand planning process generating rolling 12-18 month forecasts for 500-5,000 SKUs across multiple product families, channels, and geographies. The process integrates statistical forecasting models, sales intelligence, marketing event calendars, and customer collaboration inputs into a consensus demand plan reviewed monthly through the S&OP cycle.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Forecast accuracy (MAPE): <20% at SKU-month level
- Forecast bias: within ±5%
- S&OP cycle time: <5 business days
- Inventory turns improvement: >10% year-over-year
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Statistical baseline** generated monthly using 24-month rolling history
- **Promotional lifts** added as overrides with documented assumptions
- **Product lifecycle** adjustments (introduction, growth, maturity, decline) applied to baseline
- Forecasts locked **5 days** before S&OP executive review — no changes after lock

### SLA & Performance
- Statistical forecast published by **Day 5** of month
- Demand review meeting by **Day 10**
- Consensus plan locked by **Day 15**
- Executive S&OP by **Day 20**
human_approvals:
### Demand Consensus Override
- **Trigger**: Sales forecast deviates >30% from statistical baseline for a product family
- **Reviewer**: Demand Planning Manager + Sales Director
- **Actions**: Review evidence (pipeline, orders, market intelligence), agree consensus number, document assumptions
- **Timeout**: 48 hours (within S&OP calendar window)

### New Product Forecast Approval
- **Trigger**: New product launch within 6 months — no historical data
- **Reviewer**: Product Manager + Demand Planner + Finance
- **Actions**: Analogous product modeling, market sizing, pre-launch order analysis, set initial forecast
- **Timeout**: Aligned with stage-gate NPI timeline

name: New Product Introduction (NPI)
summary: Stage-gate NPI process from concept through production ramp-up with cross-functional milestone reviews.
description:
A new product introduction process managing 10-30 active NPI projects through a 5-stage gate framework: Concept, Feasibility, Development, Validation, and Launch. The process coordinates engineering, manufacturing, quality, procurement, and commercial teams to bring products from design concept through production readiness.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Time-to-market: <12 months (standard), <6 months (fast-track)
- First-time-right at production validation: >85%
- NPI project on-budget: >90%
- Launch-month quality (warranty/returns): <1%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Product safety certifications** (UL, CE, FCC) obtained before production
- **FDA 510(k)/PMA** clearance for medical device products
- **REACH/RoHS** material compliance documentation
- **Patent clearance** completed before Gate 3

### Business Rules
- **No gate skipping** — all deliverables must be complete for gate passage
- **Prototype builds** must use production-intent tooling and processes
- **Bill of Materials** frozen at Gate 4 — changes require formal ECO
- **Manufacturing readiness** assessment score >85% required for Gate 5 (Launch)

### SLA & Performance
- Gate reviews completed within **5 business days** of request
- Engineering prototypes within **4 weeks** of design freeze
- Production validation (PVT) run within **2 weeks** of tooling qualification
human_approvals:
### Stage Gate Review
- **Trigger**: Project reaches gate milestone (deliverables complete)
- **Reviewer**: NPI Steering Committee (VP Engineering, VP Operations, VP Sales, VP Quality)
- **Actions**: Review deliverables, assess risk register, approve budget for next phase, Go/Kill/Recycle decision
- **Timeout**: Gate review scheduled within 5 business days of deliverable completion

### Design Change After Validation Gate
- **Trigger**: Engineering change required after Gate 4 (Validation) approval
- **Reviewer**: NPI Program Manager + VP Engineering + Quality Director
- **Actions**: Full impact assessment (cost, timeline, tooling, regulatory), re-validation plan
- **Timeout**: 48 hours for impact assessment → Steering Committee if >$50K impact

name: Warehouse & Inventory Management
summary: Inbound/outbound warehouse operations, pick-pack-ship, cycle counting, and inventory accuracy management.
description:
A warehouse management process handling 2,000-10,000 transactions per day across receiving, putaway, storage, picking, packing, and shipping operations. The process maintains inventory accuracy through directed putaway, wave planning, RF-guided picking, and continuous cycle counting programs.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Inventory accuracy: >99.5%
- Order picking accuracy: >99.8%
- Dock-to-stock time: <4 hours
- Order ship-on-time: >98%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **OSHA** warehouse safety (forklift certification, ergonomics, fall protection)
- **DOT/IATA** hazardous materials shipping regulations
- **Customs/trade** compliance for bonded warehouse operations
- **FDA** warehouse conditions for food/pharma/medical device storage

### Business Rules
- **FIFO** enforced for all perishable and date-sensitive materials
- **Cycle counting** — A items weekly, B items monthly, C items quarterly
- **Putaway** within **4 hours** of receiving completion
- **Damaged goods** segregated immediately — no commingling with saleable inventory

### SLA & Performance
- Receiving processed within **4 hours** of dock arrival
- Picks released to floor within **2 hours** of wave generation
- Same-day shipping for orders received by **2 PM** cutoff
human_approvals:
### Inventory Discrepancy Investigation
- **Trigger**: Cycle count variance >$5,000 OR perpetual vs. physical count deviation >2%
- **Reviewer**: Warehouse Supervisor + Inventory Control Analyst
- **Actions**: Recount, investigate root cause (misput, damage, theft, system error), adjust with approval
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for investigation → 48 hours for adjustment approval

### Hazardous Material Handling
- **Trigger**: Receipt or shipment of HAZMAT-classified materials
- **Reviewer**: EHS Coordinator + Warehouse Lead
- **Actions**: Verify SDS on file, confirm proper storage location, ensure shipping documentation complete
- **Timeout**: Material held until all compliance steps verified

name: Engineering Change Management
summary: Engineering change request/order process with impact analysis, cross-functional approval, and effectivity management.
description:
An engineering change management process handling 50-200 change requests per month across product design, manufacturing process, and documentation updates. The process covers change request submission, technical/commercial impact analysis, cross-functional review board, implementation planning, and effectivity tracking to ensure controlled transitions.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Average ECO cycle time: <15 business days
- Change implementation on-schedule: >90%
- Uncontrolled changes (escapes): 0
- Obsolete inventory from changes: <$50K/quarter
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **ISO 9001/IATF 16949** design and development change control requirements
- **FDA 21 CFR 820.30** design change requirements for medical devices
- **Configuration management** per customer contract requirements
- **Traceability** of changes for product liability

### Business Rules
- All changes require **documented impact analysis** before CCB review
- **BOM, drawing, and specification** updates must be synchronized at effectivity date
- **Existing inventory** disposition plan required (use-as-is, rework, scrap)
- Emergency changes permitted with **post-facto CCB ratification** within 48 hours

### SLA & Performance
- ECR screening within **2 business days**
- Impact analysis within **5 business days**
- CCB review at next scheduled meeting (weekly) or **24 hours** for emergency
human_approvals:
### Change Control Board (CCB) Review
- **Trigger**: ECR approved for formal evaluation — any product, process, or document change
- **Reviewer**: Change Control Board (Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing, Procurement, Sales)
- **Actions**: Review impact analysis, approve/reject/defer, assign effectivity, set implementation timeline
- **Timeout**: Weekly CCB meeting — emergency changes within 24 hours

### Safety/Regulatory Change Escalation
- **Trigger**: Change impacts product safety, regulatory certification, or customer-approved design
- **Reviewer**: VP Engineering + Quality Director + Regulatory Affairs
- **Actions**: Regulatory impact assessment, customer notification, re-certification planning
- **Timeout**: 48 hours for initial assessment

name: Inventory Replenishment & Optimization
summary: Demand-driven auto-replenishment with safety stock optimization, open-to-buy planning, and allocation.
description:
A retail inventory replenishment process managing perpetual inventory across 50-500 store locations and distribution centers, with auto-replenishment triggers based on demand signals, seasonal patterns, and promotional calendars. The process optimizes safety stock levels, manages open-to-buy budgets, and allocates inventory across channels.

**Key performance metrics:**
- In-stock rate: >96%
- Inventory turnover: >8x annually
- Shrinkage rate: <1.5%
- Overstock markdown exposure: <5% of inventory value
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Auto-replenishment** triggered when store inventory drops below **reorder point** (ROP)
- **Safety stock** calculated per SKU-location using demand variability and lead time
- **Open-to-buy** budget controlled monthly by category — overages require VP approval
- **Seasonal inventory** must be **80% received** 4 weeks before selling season start

### SLA & Performance
- Replenishment orders generated **nightly** from POS data
- DC fulfillment within **24 hours** of store replenishment order
- Stockout recovery within **3 business days** for core assortment items
human_approvals:
### Overstock/Excess Disposition
- **Trigger**: Weeks of supply >12 OR sell-through rate <30% at midseason
- **Reviewer**: Merchandise Planner + Category Manager
- **Actions**: Evaluate markdown timing, transfer to high-velocity stores, return to vendor, donate
- **Timeout**: Weekly review cycle during season

### New Store/Channel Allocation
- **Trigger**: New store opening OR new channel launch (marketplace, wholesale)
- **Reviewer**: Allocation Analyst + VP Merchandising
- **Actions**: Model demand using comparable stores, set initial assortment, establish replenishment parameters
- **Timeout**: Allocation plan finalized 4 weeks before opening

name: Customer Returns & Refund Processing
summary: Omnichannel returns processing with refund authorization, disposition routing, and fraud screening.
description:
A retail returns management process handling 10,000-50,000 returns per month across in-store, online (ship-back and drop-off), and marketplace channels. The process covers return eligibility validation, refund/exchange/store-credit authorization, item inspection and disposition (restock, refurbish, liquidate, recycle), and return fraud detection.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Return processing time: <24 hours (in-store), <5 business days (online)
- Refund accuracy: >99.5%
- Return fraud detection rate: >80% of flagged cases confirmed
- Restock rate: >70% of returned items
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FTC Cooling-Off Rule** for door-to-door sales returns
- **State consumer protection** laws for refund policies
- **PCI DSS** compliance for credit card refund processing
- **Sales tax** adjustment reporting for returned items

### Business Rules
- Standard return window: **30 days** with receipt, **14 days** without
- **Opened electronics** subject to **15% restocking fee** (disclosed at purchase)
- Refund issued to **original payment method** within **5 business days**
- Return shipping paid by **retailer** for defective items, **customer** for change-of-mind

### SLA & Performance
- In-store refund processed at **point of return** (<5 minutes)
- Online refund initiated within **24 hours** of warehouse receipt
- Disposition decision within **48 hours** of item inspection
human_approvals:
### High-Value Return Authorization
- **Trigger**: Return value >$500 OR item on high-fraud watchlist
- **Reviewer**: Returns Supervisor + Loss Prevention
- **Actions**: Verify purchase history, inspect item condition, check return pattern for customer, approve/deny
- **Timeout**: 2 hours for in-store, 24 hours for online

### Return Policy Exception
- **Trigger**: Return outside policy window OR missing receipt/proof of purchase
- **Reviewer**: Store Manager (in-store) or Customer Service Lead (online)
- **Actions**: Evaluate customer history, loyalty status, goodwill consideration, approve with documentation
- **Timeout**: Immediate for in-store; 24 hours online

name: Pricing & Markdown Optimization
summary: Dynamic pricing, promotional pricing rules, markdown cadence management, and competitive price matching.
description:
A retail pricing and markdown optimization process managing price lifecycle for 10,000-100,000 SKUs across regular price, promotional events, clearance markdowns, and competitive responses. The process uses demand elasticity models, margin targets, and sell-through velocity to optimize pricing decisions at SKU-store-channel granularity.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Gross margin rate: target by category (40-65%)
- Promotional lift: >2x base sales during promotion
- Markdown sell-through: >90% of clearance inventory
- Price perception score: top quartile vs. competition
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Initial markup** (IMU) must meet category margin target at time of receipt
- Markdowns follow defined **cadence**: 25% at week 8, 40% at week 12, 60% at week 16
- **Price changes** effective on a consistent schedule (weekly on Wednesdays)
- **MAP (Minimum Advertised Price)** compliance enforced for vendor-restricted brands

### SLA & Performance
- Price changes published to POS/ecommerce within **2 hours** of approval
- Competitive price reviews completed **weekly**
- Promotional pricing loaded **48 hours** before event start
human_approvals:
### Deep Discount Authorization
- **Trigger**: Markdown exceeds 50% of original retail OR margin drops below cost
- **Reviewer**: Category Director + VP Merchandising
- **Actions**: Validate liquidation rationale, assess brand impact, approve final markdown or alternative disposition
- **Timeout**: 48 hours → auto-approve if within pre-authorized clearance program

### Competitive Price Match Escalation
- **Trigger**: Competitor price <10% below current retail on key item
- **Reviewer**: Pricing Analyst + Category Manager
- **Actions**: Verify competitor price, assess margin impact, approve match/partial match/hold
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for key items

name: Omnichannel Order Management
summary: Distributed order management with BOPIS, ship-from-store, drop-ship, and marketplace fulfillment.
description:
A retail omnichannel order management process orchestrating 20,000-100,000 orders per day across web, mobile, marketplace, and in-store channels. The distributed order management (DOM) system optimizes fulfillment routing across DCs, stores (ship-from-store, BOPIS/curbside), and drop-ship vendors based on inventory availability, proximity, cost, and SLA requirements.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Order fill rate: >98%
- BOPIS ready within: 2 hours
- Ship-from-store cycle time: <24 hours
- Order cancellation rate: <3%
- Shipping cost per order: decreasing trend
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **PCI DSS** for payment data handling across all channels
- **Sales tax nexus** compliance (multi-state collection via Streamlined Sales Tax)
- **Consumer protection** — accurate delivery date promises
- **ADA compliance** for digital ordering interfaces

### Business Rules
- **Inventory reservation** held for **30 minutes** during checkout
- **BOPIS orders** picked and staged within **2 hours** — customer notification via SMS/email
- **Ship-from-store** orders shipped same day if received before **2 PM** local time
- **Split shipment** threshold: allow up to **3 shipments** per order, combine if cost-efficient

### SLA & Performance
- Standard shipping: **3-5 business days**
- Express shipping: **next business day**
- BOPIS ready notification: within **2 hours**
- Order status updates at every **fulfillment milestone**
human_approvals:
### Fulfillment Exception Routing
- **Trigger**: Primary fulfillment node cannot fulfill (stockout, capacity, closure)
- **Reviewer**: Automated DOM engine → manual review if all nodes fail
- **Actions**: Re-route to next-best node, split order, notify customer of delay, offer alternatives
- **Timeout**: 30 minutes automated re-routing → 2 hours for manual intervention

### Fraud/Risk Order Hold
- **Trigger**: Order risk score >75 OR billing/shipping address mismatch OR velocity check failure
- **Reviewer**: Fraud Analyst
- **Actions**: Verify order, contact customer, release or cancel, update fraud rules
- **Timeout**: 4 hours during business hours → order auto-cancelled after 24 hours

name: Store Operations Management
summary: Daily store operations including opening/closing procedures, workforce scheduling, and task execution tracking.
description:
A retail store operations process managing daily workflows for 50-500 store locations including opening/closing procedures, cash management, associate task assignments, customer service standards, and compliance checklists. The process ensures consistent execution of brand standards across all locations through digital task management and real-time visibility.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Task completion rate: >95%
- Cash variance: <$5 per register per day
- Customer satisfaction (NPS): >50
- Labor cost as % of sales: within budget ±2%
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Opening checklist** completed before store opens to customers
- **Cash deposits** made daily — no more than **$3,000** in safe overnight
- **Associate scheduling** published **2 weeks** in advance per predictive scheduling laws
- **Planogram compliance** audited weekly by visual merchandising standards

### SLA & Performance
- Opening tasks completed within **30 minutes** of associate clock-in
- Customer greeting within **30 seconds** of store entry
- Closing reconciliation completed within **30 minutes** of store close
human_approvals:
### Cash Discrepancy Investigation
- **Trigger**: Register variance >$25 OR safe count discrepancy >$100
- **Reviewer**: Store Manager + District Loss Prevention
- **Actions**: Review camera footage, audit transactions, interview associates, document findings
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for investigation → escalate to LP Director if >$500

### Store Emergency Protocol
- **Trigger**: Safety incident, severe weather, system outage, or security threat
- **Reviewer**: Store Manager + Regional Manager
- **Actions**: Activate emergency procedures, ensure customer/associate safety, notify corporate operations
- **Timeout**: Immediate response — situational

name: Loyalty Program Management
summary: Points accrual, tier management, redemption processing, and personalized offer delivery.
description:
A retail loyalty program management process serving 2-10 million active members across tiered reward structures. The process handles points accrual from purchases, tier qualification and benefits management, redemption processing, personalized offer targeting, and program financial liability tracking.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Active member rate: >40% of customer base
- Redemption rate: 15-25% of points earned
- Member spend lift vs. non-members: >20%
- Program ROI: >3x marketing cost
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Points earn**: 1 point per $1 spent (base), multipliers for tier/category promotions
- **Points expiration**: 12 months of account inactivity
- **Tier qualification** reset annually based on rolling 12-month spend
- **Redemption** minimum: 500 points ($5 value) — no cash-out option

### SLA & Performance
- Points posted within **24 hours** of transaction
- Tier status updated **real-time** upon qualifying transaction
- Personalized offers delivered within **48 hours** of qualifying behavior
human_approvals:
### Points Liability Threshold
- **Trigger**: Outstanding points liability exceeds budget threshold OR breakage rate drops below forecast
- **Reviewer**: Loyalty Program Manager + CFO/Finance Director
- **Actions**: Adjust earn rates, introduce expiration accelerators, launch redemption promotions
- **Timeout**: Quarterly review cycle

### Fraud/Abuse Detection
- **Trigger**: Unusual point accumulation pattern OR multiple accounts linked to same identity
- **Reviewer**: Loyalty Operations + Fraud Team
- **Actions**: Freeze account, investigate transactions, merge/deactivate duplicate accounts
- **Timeout**: 48 hours for investigation

name: Vehicle Recall Management
summary: Safety recall identification, parts sourcing, dealer notification, owner outreach, and completion tracking.
description:
An automotive recall management process coordinating safety-related and compliance recalls from defect identification through remedy completion. The process handles NHTSA reporting, parts procurement and distribution, dealer notification, owner outreach campaigns, and completion rate tracking across a network of 500-3,000 authorized dealers.

**Key performance metrics:**
- NHTSA reporting compliance: 100% within 5 business days
- Parts availability at dealer launch: >90%
- Owner notification: 100% within 60 days
- Recall completion rate: >80% within 18 months
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **NHTSA 49 CFR Part 573** defect and noncompliance reporting
- **TREAD Act** early warning reporting requirements
- **Owner notification** per 49 USC §30118 (first-class mail within 60 days)
- **Quarterly completion reports** to NHTSA until adequate completion rate

### Business Rules
- **Stop-sale** issued for affected unsold vehicles within **24 hours** of recall decision
- Dealer **repair reimbursement** at standard warranty labor rate + parts at cost
- Owner remedy provided at **no charge** regardless of vehicle age or mileage
- **VIN lookup tool** updated on brand website within **48 hours** of recall launch

### SLA & Performance
- NHTSA report filed within **5 business days**
- Owner notification mailed within **60 days**
- Dealer technical bulletin published within **10 business days**
human_approvals:
### Safety Defect Determination
- **Trigger**: Engineering analysis confirms safety-related defect OR NHTSA investigation opened
- **Reviewer**: VP Engineering + General Counsel + VP Quality
- **Actions**: Confirm defect population, determine remedy, prepare NHTSA Part 573 report, executive briefing
- **Timeout**: NHTSA Part 573 report within **5 business days** of determination

### Remedy Parts Shortage
- **Trigger**: Parts supply insufficient to meet dealer demand within 30 days
- **Reviewer**: Supply Chain Director + Recall Program Manager
- **Actions**: Prioritize production, implement interim remedy if available, communicate timeline to dealers
- **Timeout**: Weekly status review until supply meets demand

name: Warranty Claims Processing
summary: Dealer warranty claim submission, technical validation, cost review, and reimbursement processing.
description:
An automotive warranty claims process handling 50,000-200,000 claims per month from authorized dealer networks. The process covers claim submission, technical validation against warranty coverage policies, cost auditing (parts + labor), payment authorization, and trend analysis for quality improvement feedback.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Claim processing cycle: <5 business days
- First-submission acceptance rate: >90%
- Claims cost per unit (CPU): trending within budget
- Fraud/abuse detection rate: >75% of flagged cases confirmed
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act** compliance for written warranties
- **FTC Used Car Rule** for pre-owned vehicle warranties
- **State lemon laws** — warranty repair attempt tracking
- **GAAP** warranty reserve accounting requirements

### Business Rules
- Claims must be submitted within **30 days** of repair completion
- **Parts return** required for claims >$200 (retained for failure analysis)
- **Labor time** validated against standard repair time (SRT) allowances
- **Goodwill claims** (out of warranty) require district manager approval

### SLA & Performance
- Standard claims processed within **5 business days**
- Dealer reimbursement within **15 business days** of approval
- Parts return compliance audited **monthly**
human_approvals:
### High-Cost Claim Review
- **Trigger**: Claim amount >$5,000 OR engine/transmission replacement
- **Reviewer**: Warranty Technical Specialist + Regional Service Manager
- **Actions**: Verify failure mode, review repair order, inspect failed part (if required), approve/reject
- **Timeout**: 3 business days

### Warranty Fraud Investigation
- **Trigger**: Statistical anomaly detected (claim frequency, parts usage, labor time) at dealer level
- **Reviewer**: Warranty Audit Team + Dealer Relations
- **Actions**: Desk audit, on-site audit, parts inspection, chargeback or dealer termination if confirmed
- **Timeout**: 30 days for investigation

name: Vehicle Production Sequencing
summary: Build-to-order production sequencing with constraint management, JIT supplier coordination, and line balancing.
description:
An automotive production sequencing process managing the daily build sequence for 500-1,500 vehicles per day on a mixed-model assembly line. The process translates customer orders and stock orders into an optimized build sequence that balances labor content, option/color variety, constraint rules (paint blocks, option exclusions), and just-in-time supplier synchronization.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Sequence stability (frozen zone adherence): >95%
- Build-to-schedule: >98%
- Line stops due to sequencing issues: <0.1%
- JIT supplier on-time delivery: >99.5%
business_constraints:
### Business Rules
- **Sequence frozen** 3 days before build — no changes without Production Director approval
- **Paint block** rules: maximum 3 consecutive same-color vehicles, minimum 2 between specialty colors
- **JIT suppliers** receive sequence broadcast **6 hours** before unit reaches their station
- **High-option vehicles** spaced minimum **3 units apart** for labor balance

### SLA & Performance
- Sequence published **5 days** before production date (tentative)
- Sequence frozen **3 days** before production (firm)
- JIT supplier notification **6 hours** before line position
human_approvals:
### Sequence Break Request
- **Trigger**: Quality hold, parts shortage, or customer priority change requires sequence modification within frozen window
- **Reviewer**: Production Control Manager + Quality (if quality-related)
- **Actions**: Evaluate line impact, identify swap candidate, update supplier JIT calls, approve/deny
- **Timeout**: 2 hours — production impact is immediate

### Constraint Violation Override
- **Trigger**: Sequencing algorithm cannot resolve all constraints (paint block, labor balance, supplier capacity)
- **Reviewer**: Production Planner + Industrial Engineering
- **Actions**: Identify least-impact constraint to relax, document override, notify affected station
- **Timeout**: 4 hours before sequence freeze deadline

name: Freight Booking & Management
summary: Freight booking, carrier selection, rate negotiation, shipment tracking, and delivery confirmation.
description:
A freight management process handling 5,000-20,000 shipments per month across FTL, LTL, intermodal, and parcel modes. The process covers booking requests, carrier selection (rate/service/capacity optimization), documentation generation (BOL, commercial invoice), real-time tracking, exception management, and freight audit/payment.

**Key performance metrics:**
- On-time pickup: >97%
- On-time delivery: >95%
- Freight cost per unit shipped: within budget ±3%
- Claims ratio: <0.5% of shipments
- Carrier compliance score: >90%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FMCSA** carrier safety ratings and operating authority verification
- **DOT** hazmat shipping regulations (49 CFR) for regulated materials
- **Customs** documentation for international shipments (commercial invoice, packing list)
- **Hours of Service** compliance monitoring for carrier selection

### Business Rules
- **Carrier selection** based on: contracted rate > service level > capacity > transit time
- **BOL** generated automatically from order data — manual override requires supervisor approval
- **Insurance** coverage verified for all shipments >$50,000 value
- **Freight audit** performed on 100% of invoices before payment

### SLA & Performance
- Booking confirmation within **2 hours** of request
- Tracking updates every **4 hours** during transit
- Freight invoice processing within **5 business days**
human_approvals:
### Shipment Exception Management
- **Trigger**: Missed pickup, transit delay >24 hours, damage reported, or refused delivery
- **Reviewer**: Logistics Coordinator + Customer Service
- **Actions**: Contact carrier, arrange recovery/re-delivery, notify customer, file claim if applicable
- **Timeout**: 2 hours for critical shipments → 8 hours for standard

### Carrier Rate Deviation
- **Trigger**: Invoice amount >10% above quoted rate
- **Reviewer**: Freight Audit Analyst + Procurement
- **Actions**: Validate accessorial charges, dispute overcharges, negotiate credit
- **Timeout**: 30 days per carrier payment terms

name: Fleet Management & Optimization
summary: Vehicle lifecycle management, driver compliance, fuel optimization, and DOT regulatory adherence.
description:
A fleet management process overseeing 200-2,000 commercial vehicles including trucks, vans, and specialized equipment. The process covers vehicle acquisition/disposal lifecycle, preventive maintenance scheduling, driver qualification file management, ELD/HOS compliance monitoring, fuel management, and accident/incident tracking.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Vehicle uptime: >95%
- DOT compliance rate: 100% (CSA scores in green)
- Fuel cost per mile: within budget
- Accident rate: <2 per million miles
- PM compliance: >98%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FMCSA** ELD mandate and Hours of Service regulations
- **DOT** vehicle inspection, repair, and maintenance standards (49 CFR 396)
- **CDL** driver qualification file requirements (49 CFR 391)
- **EPA/CARB** emissions compliance for commercial vehicles
- **IFTA** fuel tax reporting for interstate operations

### Business Rules
- **Pre-trip inspections** (DVIR) required before every trip
- **Preventive maintenance** per OEM schedule — no deferrals beyond 10% of interval
- **Driver qualification files** audited annually — missing documents = out of service
- **Fuel card** usage monitored — transactions >$500 require supervisor verification

### SLA & Performance
- Breakdown roadside assistance response within **2 hours**
- PM turnaround within **24 hours** (scheduled downtime)
- New driver onboarding/qualification within **5 business days**
human_approvals:
### DOT Compliance Violation
- **Trigger**: Driver HOS violation, vehicle out-of-service condition, or CSA score degradation
- **Reviewer**: Fleet Safety Manager + Compliance Officer
- **Actions**: Remove vehicle/driver from service, corrective action, driver retraining, FMCSA DataQs challenge if appropriate
- **Timeout**: Immediate for safety-critical → 24 hours for documentation corrections

### Vehicle Replacement Decision
- **Trigger**: Vehicle reaches replacement threshold (mileage, age, maintenance cost)
- **Reviewer**: Fleet Manager + Finance Director
- **Actions**: Total cost of ownership analysis, spec replacement vehicle, budget approval, disposal plan
- **Timeout**: Quarterly fleet review cycle

name: Customs Clearance & Trade Compliance
summary: Import/export customs documentation, tariff classification, duty calculation, and regulatory compliance.
description:
A customs clearance process managing 2,000-10,000 import/export declarations per month across multiple countries and trade agreements. The process covers tariff classification (HTS/HS codes), customs valuation, duty/tax calculation, license/permit management, restricted party screening, and post-entry audit compliance.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Customs clearance time: <48 hours (standard), <4 hours (expedited)
- Classification accuracy: >99%
- Duty savings from FTA utilization: >$500K annually
- Compliance audit findings: 0 penalties
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **US CBP** entry requirements and Customs Modernization Act
- **C-TPAT/AEO** trusted trader program compliance
- **Export Administration Regulations (EAR)** and ITAR for controlled items
- **OFAC** sanctions screening for all parties
- **FTA/preferential trade** rules of origin certification

### Business Rules
- **Restricted party screening** on 100% of transactions before shipment
- **FTA eligibility** evaluated for all qualifying shipments to minimize duty
- **Import entries** filed within **10 days** of arrival (15 days for vessel)
- **Record retention**: all customs documents retained for **5 years** (US) or per local law

### SLA & Performance
- Pre-clearance filing **24 hours** before vessel/aircraft arrival
- Standard clearance within **48 hours** of arrival
- Expedited clearance within **4 hours** for time-sensitive shipments
human_approvals:
### Restricted Party Screening Hit
- **Trigger**: Shipper, consignee, or end-user matches restricted/denied party list
- **Reviewer**: Trade Compliance Officer + Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Hold shipment, verify match (false positive check), escalate true match to BIS/OFAC, determine action
- **Timeout**: Immediate hold → 24 hours for false positive resolution → legal if confirmed match

### Classification Dispute
- **Trigger**: Customs authority challenges tariff classification at import
- **Reviewer**: Trade Compliance Manager + Customs Broker + Product Engineering
- **Actions**: Prepare technical justification, ruling request, protest filing if duty impact >$10,000
- **Timeout**: Per customs authority protest timeline (typically 90-180 days)

name: Last-Mile Delivery Optimization
summary: Route optimization, delivery scheduling, proof-of-delivery, and customer communication for final-mile delivery.
description:
A last-mile delivery operation managing 5,000-50,000 deliveries per day across urban, suburban, and rural zones. The process optimizes multi-stop routes dynamically, manages delivery time windows, captures proof-of-delivery (photo, signature, GPS), and handles exceptions (access issues, failed deliveries, customer rescheduling).

**Key performance metrics:**
- On-time delivery: >95% within promised window
- First-attempt delivery success: >90%
- Cost per delivery: within budget ±5%
- Customer delivery satisfaction: >4.5/5.0
- Stops per route: optimized for 120-180 per day
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **DOT** vehicle safety for delivery fleet
- **Age verification** for alcohol, tobacco, and restricted product deliveries
- **Noise ordinances** for early morning/late evening delivery windows
- **Data privacy** (GDPR, CCPA) for customer delivery data and photos

### Business Rules
- **Route optimization** re-run at **6 AM** incorporating overnight order changes
- **Delivery windows**: standard (8 AM-8 PM), premium (2-hour window), same-day (order by noon)
- **Safe drop** authorized for packages <$200 with no signature requirement
- **Driver break compliance** — mandatory 30-minute break per 6 hours driving

### SLA & Performance
- Route plans published to drivers by **6:30 AM**
- Customer delivery notification **30 minutes** before arrival
- Proof-of-delivery uploaded within **5 minutes** of completion
human_approvals:
### Failed Delivery Reattempt
- **Trigger**: Delivery attempt unsuccessful (no access, customer not home, address issue)
- **Reviewer**: Dispatch Coordinator (automated for standard, manual for high-value)
- **Actions**: Leave notice, schedule reattempt, offer customer redirect to pickup point or new time slot
- **Timeout**: Reattempt within next business day → hold at depot after 3 attempts

### High-Value/Signature-Required Override
- **Trigger**: Package value >$500 OR hazmat/age-restricted delivery without recipient present
- **Reviewer**: Driver → Dispatch Supervisor
- **Actions**: Cannot safe-drop — must obtain signature or return to depot, no neighbor release
- **Timeout**: Immediate decision at doorstep

name: IT Change Management (ITIL)
summary: ITIL change management with RFC submission, CAB review, implementation scheduling, and post-implementation review.
description:
An IT change management process handling 200-1,000 change requests per month across infrastructure, application, and network changes. The process follows ITIL v4 practices with change categorization (standard, normal, emergency), risk assessment, Change Advisory Board review, implementation scheduling, and post-implementation verification.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Change success rate: >95%
- Emergency change ratio: <10% of total changes
- Average change lead time: <5 business days (normal)
- Change-related incidents: <5% of all incidents
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **SOX** IT general controls for financial system changes
- **PCI DSS** change management requirements for cardholder data environments
- **HIPAA** technical safeguard change documentation
- **SOC 2** change management control evidence

### Business Rules
- **Standard changes** pre-approved — no CAB needed (e.g., password resets, patch Tuesday)
- **Change freeze** periods enforced during peak business (month-end, holiday)
- **Backout plan** required for all normal and emergency changes
- Changes to **production** require **peer-reviewed** implementation script/runbook

### SLA & Performance
- Standard change: implemented within **24 hours** of request
- Normal change: CAB review at next meeting, implement within **5 business days**
- Emergency change: implemented within **4 hours**, documented within **24 hours**
human_approvals:
### Change Advisory Board (CAB) Review
- **Trigger**: Normal change request submitted with risk assessment score >medium
- **Reviewer**: CAB (IT Director, Security, Infrastructure, Application leads)
- **Actions**: Review risk/impact analysis, approve implementation window, assign resources, set backout plan
- **Timeout**: Weekly CAB meeting — emergency CAB within 2 hours

### Emergency Change Authorization
- **Trigger**: Production outage or critical security vulnerability requiring immediate change
- **Reviewer**: Emergency CAB (on-call manager + security lead)
- **Actions**: Verbal approval, expedited implementation, post-facto documentation within 24h
- **Timeout**: 30 minutes for approval decision

name: Cybersecurity Incident Response
summary: SOC incident triage, containment, eradication, forensic investigation, and recovery per NIST CSF.
description:
A cybersecurity incident response process managing security events from detection through recovery and lessons learned. The process covers SIEM alert triage, severity classification, containment actions, forensic investigation, eradication, system recovery, and regulatory notification across an environment of 10,000-50,000 endpoints and 500+ cloud services.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Mean time to detect (MTTD): <15 minutes
- Mean time to contain (MTTC): <1 hour (critical), <4 hours (high)
- False positive rate: <30%
- Incident closure within SLA: >95%
- Regulatory notification compliance: 100%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **NIST CSF/SP 800-61** incident handling guidance
- **GDPR Article 33/34** breach notification (72 hours to DPA)
- **SEC cybersecurity disclosure** rules (4 business days for material incidents)
- **State breach notification** laws (50 states, varies)
- **PCI DSS Incident Response** plan requirements

### Business Rules
- All **P1/P2 incidents** require post-incident review within **5 business days**
- **Evidence preservation** — forensic images before any remediation
- **Containment** authorized without approval for automated playbook actions (isolate host, block IP)
- **External forensics** firm engaged for all confirmed breaches

### SLA & Performance
- Alert triage within **15 minutes** of SIEM notification
- Critical containment within **1 hour**
- Root cause analysis within **72 hours** of containment
- Post-incident report within **5 business days**
human_approvals:
### Critical Severity Escalation
- **Trigger**: Confirmed data breach, ransomware, APT, or critical system compromise
- **Reviewer**: CISO + Incident Commander + Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Activate incident command, authorize containment measures, assess breach scope, initiate legal/regulatory response
- **Timeout**: 15 minutes for initial response team assembly

### Breach Notification Decision
- **Trigger**: Personal data or regulated data confirmed exposed
- **Reviewer**: CISO + General Counsel + Privacy Officer + CEO
- **Actions**: Assess notification obligations, prepare notifications, engage external counsel, notify regulators
- **Timeout**: Per regulatory timelines (GDPR: 72 hours, state breach laws vary)

name: Software Release Management
summary: Release pipeline management with build validation, staging promotion, production deployment gates, and rollback procedures.
description:
A software release management process governing 50-200 production deployments per month across web applications, APIs, microservices, and mobile apps. The process orchestrates CI/CD pipelines with quality gates (unit tests, integration tests, security scans, performance benchmarks), staged rollouts (canary, blue-green), and automated rollback capabilities.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Deployment frequency: multiple per day (microservices), weekly (monoliths)
- Deployment failure rate: <5%
- Mean time to recovery (MTTR): <30 minutes
- Lead time for changes: <24 hours
- Change failure rate: <15%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **SOX** segregation of duties for financial application releases
- **PCI DSS** change management for cardholder data environment
- **SOC 2** release management controls and evidence
- **FDA 21 CFR Part 11** validation for medical device software

### Business Rules
- All releases require **passing quality gates**: unit tests (>90% coverage), SAST scan (no critical), DAST scan
- **Feature flags** required for all new user-facing features
- **Canary deployment** (5% traffic) for 30 minutes before full rollout
- **Rollback** triggered automatically if error rate >2x baseline within 15 minutes

### SLA & Performance
- Build pipeline execution: **<15 minutes**
- Staging validation: **<2 hours**
- Production rollout: **<30 minutes** (canary + full)
- Rollback execution: **<5 minutes**
human_approvals:
### Production Deployment Approval
- **Trigger**: Release candidate passes all quality gates and is ready for production
- **Reviewer**: Release Manager + QA Lead + Service Owner
- **Actions**: Verify test results, confirm rollback plan, check deployment window, authorize release
- **Timeout**: 4 hours during business hours → next business day if after hours

### Hotfix Emergency Release
- **Trigger**: Critical production bug or security vulnerability requiring immediate fix
- **Reviewer**: On-call Engineer + Engineering Manager
- **Actions**: Abbreviated test cycle (critical path only), deploy with monitoring, full regression within 24h
- **Timeout**: 2 hours for fix + deploy

name: IT Service Request Fulfillment
summary: IT service catalog request intake, approval routing, automated provisioning, and fulfillment tracking.
description:
An IT service request fulfillment process handling 2,000-10,000 requests per month from a self-service catalog spanning hardware provisioning, software licensing, access management, and workplace services. The process automates routine fulfillment (password resets, group membership) while routing complex requests through appropriate approval chains.

**Key performance metrics:**
- First-contact resolution: >70%
- Automation rate: >50% of requests
- Average fulfillment time: <24 hours (standard)
- User satisfaction (CSAT): >90%
- SLA compliance: >95%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **SOX** access provisioning controls for financial systems
- **HIPAA** minimum necessary access for healthcare data
- **SOC 2** access management and logging requirements
- **GDPR** right of access and data subject provisioning

### Business Rules
- **Standard requests** (pre-approved catalog items) fulfilled without additional approval
- **New hire provisioning** completed by employee's **first day** (manager submits 5 days prior)
- **Access reviews** for all provisioned access every **90 days**
- **Terminated employee** access revoked within **4 hours** of HR notification

### SLA & Performance
- Password reset: **<15 minutes** (automated)
- Standard hardware: **<3 business days**
- Software installation: **<24 hours**
- Custom/complex requests: **<5 business days**
human_approvals:
### Budget/License Approval
- **Trigger**: Request involves cost >$500 OR software license allocation
- **Reviewer**: Requestor's Manager + IT Finance (if >$5,000)
- **Actions**: Verify budget availability, approve license allocation, confirm business justification
- **Timeout**: 48 hours → auto-escalate to next-level manager

### Elevated Access Request
- **Trigger**: Request for admin/root access, production database access, or privileged role
- **Reviewer**: Security Team + System Owner
- **Actions**: Verify business need, apply least-privilege principle, set time-bound access, schedule review
- **Timeout**: 24 hours → escalate to CISO for persistent access requests

name: Network Fault Management
summary: NOC fault detection, alarm correlation, diagnosis, service restoration, and reason-for-outage reporting.
description:
A telecom network fault management process operating from a 24x7 Network Operations Center (NOC) monitoring 50,000-200,000 network elements across RAN, transport, core, and IP/MPLS networks. The process covers alarm ingestion, correlation and suppression, fault isolation, dispatch coordination, service restoration, and customer impact analysis.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Mean time to detect (MTTD): <5 minutes
- Mean time to restore (MTTR): <2 hours (major), <30 minutes (critical)
- Network availability: >99.99%
- Customer-affecting alarms resolved within SLA: >95%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FCC** network outage reporting (>30 minutes, >900,000 user-minutes)
- **E911/NG911** service restoration priority requirements
- **CPNI** customer data protection during fault resolution
- **SLA contractual** obligations for enterprise/government customers

### Business Rules
- **Alarm suppression** rules: child alarms auto-suppressed when parent fault identified
- **Escalation matrix**: P1 → 15 min, P2 → 30 min, P3 → 2 hours, P4 → 8 hours
- **Field dispatch** only after remote troubleshooting exhausted (cost avoidance)
- **RFO (Reason for Outage)** report required within **5 business days** for major incidents

### SLA & Performance
- Critical fault response: **<5 minutes**
- MTTR critical: **<30 minutes**
- MTTR major: **<2 hours**
- FCC outage report filed within **120 minutes** of discovery
human_approvals:
### Major Incident Declaration
- **Trigger**: >1,000 customers affected OR core network element failure OR SLA breach imminent
- **Reviewer**: NOC Manager + Network Engineering Lead
- **Actions**: Activate major incident bridge, mobilize field teams, initiate customer communication, executive notification
- **Timeout**: 15 minutes for bridge activation → VP notification at 1 hour

### Emergency Change for Restoration
- **Trigger**: Fault restoration requires network configuration change
- **Reviewer**: NOC Shift Lead + Network Engineering (verbal approval)
- **Actions**: Execute change, monitor restoration, document as emergency change, post-facto CAB review
- **Timeout**: Immediate — service restoration priority

name: Order-to-Activate Provisioning
summary: Service order processing, network resource assignment, provisioning, activation, and customer confirmation.
description:
A telecom order-to-activate process handling 10,000-50,000 service orders per month for residential and business customers across mobile, broadband, voice, and managed services. The process covers order entry/validation, credit check, network design (business), resource assignment, service provisioning, activation testing, and customer notification.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Order-to-activate cycle time: <24 hours (consumer), <5 days (business)
- Order fallout rate: <5%
- First-time-right activation: >95%
- Customer-reported activation issues: <3%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FCC** local competition rules (interconnection, number assignment)
- **LNP (Local Number Portability)** porting timelines (simple port: 1 business day)
- **CPNI** verification before account changes
- **Universal Service Fund** contribution and reporting

### Business Rules
- **Credit check** required for all new customers and plan upgrades with device financing
- **Address validation** against USPS/ALI database before order acceptance
- **Number porting** — simple ports within **1 business day**, complex within **4 business days**
- **Activation testing** mandatory before order completion (dial tone, data throughput)

### SLA & Performance
- Consumer service activation: within **24 hours** of order
- Business service activation: within **5 business days** (standard)
- Order status updates: every **4 hours** during provisioning
human_approvals:
### Order Fallout Resolution
- **Trigger**: Automated provisioning fails due to resource conflict, address issue, or system error
- **Reviewer**: Service Delivery Specialist
- **Actions**: Diagnose fallout reason, manual resource assignment, retry provisioning, update customer on delay
- **Timeout**: 4 hours for consumer orders, 8 hours for business

### Complex/Custom Service Design
- **Trigger**: Business order requires custom network design (MPLS, dedicated fiber, SD-WAN)
- **Reviewer**: Network Design Engineer + Solution Architect
- **Actions**: Site survey, network design, cost estimate, customer approval, project plan
- **Timeout**: 5 business days for initial design proposal

name: Billing Dispute Resolution
summary: Customer billing inquiry investigation, charge validation, credit/adjustment authorization, and root cause fix.
description:
A telecom billing dispute resolution process handling 5,000-20,000 billing inquiries per month across consumer and business accounts. The process covers dispute intake, charge validation against CDRs/usage records, credit/adjustment calculation, authorization routing, root cause identification, and systemic issue escalation to prevent recurrence.

**Key performance metrics:**
- First-call resolution rate: >60%
- Average dispute resolution time: <5 business days
- Credit accuracy: >99%
- Repeat dispute rate: <10%
- Revenue leakage from erroneous credits: <0.1%
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Truth in Billing** (FCC) — clear, non-misleading billing practices
- **Bill shock** protections (international roaming, data overage notifications)
- **State PUC/PSC** regulations on billing dispute resolution timelines
- **TCPA** compliance for dispute-related communications

### Business Rules
- Credits <**$50** auto-approved at frontline agent level (first occurrence)
- **Pro-rata credits** calculated based on actual service disruption duration
- Disputes older than **90 days** require manager approval to investigate
- **Repeat disputes** (3+ in 12 months) trigger account review and root cause analysis

### SLA & Performance
- Dispute acknowledged within **24 hours**
- Resolution within **5 business days** (standard)
- Credits posted to next billing cycle (within **30 days**)
human_approvals:
### High-Value Credit Authorization
- **Trigger**: Credit/adjustment amount >$500 (consumer) or >$5,000 (business)
- **Reviewer**: Billing Supervisor + Revenue Assurance
- **Actions**: Validate dispute against CDR/usage data, confirm billing error or customer misunderstanding, authorize credit
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for consumer, 48 hours for business

### Systemic Billing Error Escalation
- **Trigger**: >10 similar disputes in 7 days OR rating/billing system defect identified
- **Reviewer**: Billing Systems Manager + Revenue Assurance Director
- **Actions**: Quantify customer impact, initiate mass credit process, deploy system fix, customer communication
- **Timeout**: 48 hours for impact assessment → 5 business days for resolution

name: Grid Maintenance & Crew Dispatch
summary: T&D infrastructure maintenance scheduling, crew dispatch, outage coordination, and work management.
description:
An electric utility grid maintenance process managing 1,000-5,000 work orders per month across transmission lines, distribution feeders, substations, and vegetation management. The process coordinates preventive maintenance schedules, corrective work dispatch, storm restoration crews, and contractor management while maintaining NERC reliability compliance.

**Key performance metrics:**
- SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index): below regulatory target
- SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index): below target
- PM completion rate: >95%
- Crew utilization: >75%
- Work order backlog: <30 days average age
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **NERC Reliability Standards** (FAC, PRC, TPL) for transmission maintenance
- **OSHA 1910.269/1926 Subpart V** electrical safety for utility workers
- **State PUC** reliability reporting (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI)
- **Vegetation management** compliance per NERC FAC-003

### Business Rules
- **Switching orders** required for all work affecting energized equipment
- **Crew qualifications** verified before dispatch (certifications, training currency)
- **Restoration priority**: hospitals/critical facilities > feeders with most customers > individual outages
- **Vegetation trim cycle**: 4 years (distribution), 3 years (transmission)

### SLA & Performance
- Emergency response: crew dispatched within **1 hour**
- Customer outage restoration: **4 hours** (urban), **8 hours** (rural)
- PM work completed within **scheduled week**
human_approvals:
### Storm/Emergency Restoration
- **Trigger**: Major weather event OR >1,000 customers without power
- **Reviewer**: Distribution Operations Manager + Emergency Coordinator
- **Actions**: Activate ICS, mobilize crews, establish restoration priorities, coordinate mutual aid
- **Timeout**: Immediate — restoration efforts until complete

### High-Voltage Work Authorization
- **Trigger**: Work on transmission (>69kV) or energized equipment
- **Reviewer**: System Operator + Qualified Electrical Worker Supervisor
- **Actions**: Issue switching orders, verify clearance/tagging, authorize work, monitor throughout
- **Timeout**: Switching completed before crew dispatched — safety hold until confirmed

name: Energy Trading & Settlement
summary: Wholesale energy market bidding, day-ahead/real-time scheduling, settlement, and imbalance management.
description:
An energy trading and settlement process managing daily participation in wholesale electricity markets (ISO/RTO). The process covers load forecasting, generation offer strategies, day-ahead market bidding, real-time dispatch optimization, financial settlement with the ISO, and imbalance cost management for a utility with 2,000-10,000 MW of generation capacity.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Forecast accuracy (day-ahead load): within ±3%
- Market revenue optimization: >$10M annual uplift vs. cost-based offers
- Settlement dispute resolution: <30 days
- Imbalance charges: <2% of total energy cost
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FERC** market-based rate authority and market behavior rules
- **NERC** reliability standards for generation dispatch
- **ISO/RTO tariff** rules for bidding, scheduling, and settlement
- **Dodd-Frank** position reporting for financial energy transactions

### Business Rules
- **Day-ahead offers** submitted by **10 AM** (per ISO deadline)
- **Real-time dispatch** followed within **5 minutes** of ISO instruction
- **Trading limits** set by Risk Committee — reviewed quarterly
- All trades **documented** with counterparty confirmation within 24 hours

### SLA & Performance
- Day-ahead bid preparation by **9 AM** for 10 AM submission
- Real-time position monitoring **continuous** (24x7)
- Settlement reconciliation within **5 business days** of ISO statement
human_approvals:
### Trading Limit Breach
- **Trigger**: Position exceeds authorized trading limit OR mark-to-market loss >threshold
- **Reviewer**: Chief Risk Officer + VP Trading
- **Actions**: Reduce position, assess exposure, report to risk committee, modify trading strategy
- **Timeout**: 1 hour for position adjustment

### Settlement Dispute
- **Trigger**: ISO/RTO settlement statement differs from internal calculation by >$100K
- **Reviewer**: Settlement Analyst + Trading Manager
- **Actions**: Reconcile meter data, verify offers/bids, file dispute with ISO per tariff timeline
- **Timeout**: Per ISO dispute timeline (typically 60-90 days)

name: Smart Meter Data Management
summary: AMI data collection, validation, estimation, billing integration, and customer usage analytics.
description:
A utility smart meter data management process handling daily interval data collection from 500,000-5,000,000 advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) endpoints. The process covers head-end system collection, VEE (Validation, Estimation, Editing), billing determinant calculation, usage analytics, outage detection, and demand response integration.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Daily read success rate: >99%
- VEE processing completion: <4 hours after collection
- Billing-quality data availability: >99.5%
- Meter-to-cash cycle time: <48 hours
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **State PUC** meter accuracy and testing requirements
- **Customer data privacy** rules for energy usage data (Green Button)
- **Net metering** regulations for solar/DER customers
- **Time-of-use** rate implementation per approved tariffs

### Business Rules
- **VEE rules** applied automatically: reasonable range, sum check, zero-usage, spike detection
- **Estimated reads** permitted for max **2 consecutive billing periods** before field visit required
- **Demand response** event data processed with **priority** over standard reads
- **Customer usage portal** updated within **24 hours** of validated read

### SLA & Performance
- Daily collection window: **midnight to 6 AM**
- VEE processing complete by **10 AM**
- Billing determinants available by **noon** for billing system
human_approvals:
### Mass Estimation Event
- **Trigger**: Read success rate drops below 95% for a geographic area (communication failure, firmware issue)
- **Reviewer**: AMI Operations Manager + Meter Data Analyst
- **Actions**: Diagnose communication issue, apply estimation algorithms, schedule re-read, escalate to vendor if systemic
- **Timeout**: 24 hours for estimation → 72 hours for root cause

### Revenue Protection Alert
- **Trigger**: Tamper flag, reverse flow anomaly, or consumption pattern inconsistent with historical usage
- **Reviewer**: Revenue Protection Analyst
- **Actions**: Analyze usage pattern, schedule field investigation, coordinate with meter shop for testing
- **Timeout**: 5 business days for field investigation

name: Government Procurement & RFP
summary: Solicitation development, bid evaluation, contract award, and administration per FAR/DFAR regulations.
description:
A government procurement process managing 100-500 solicitations per year ranging from simplified acquisitions (<$250K) to major contracts (>$10M). The process covers requirements development, market research, solicitation preparation (RFP/RFQ/IFB), proposal evaluation, source selection, award, and contract administration with full compliance to Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR).

**Key performance metrics:**
- Average procurement lead time: <90 days (simplified), <180 days (full & open)
- Protest rate: <3% of awards
- Small business goal achievement: >23% of contract dollars
- Contract file compliance: >95% on audits
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR)** and agency supplements
- **Competition in Contracting Act (CICA)** full and open competition requirements
- **Small Business Act** set-aside and subcontracting requirements
- **Buy American Act/Trade Agreements Act** domestic preference
- **Ethics in Government** — OGE financial disclosure and conflict of interest

### Business Rules
- **Micro-purchase** (<$10K): purchase card, no competition required
- **Simplified acquisition** ($10K-$250K): minimum 3 quotes, set-aside for small business
- **Full & open** (>$250K): public solicitation, formal evaluation, source selection
- **Sole source** justification requires **J&A document** approved at appropriate level

### SLA & Performance
- Solicitation published within **10 business days** of approved requirements
- Proposal evaluation within **30 days** of closing
- Award decision within **30 days** of evaluation completion
human_approvals:
### Source Selection Board Review
- **Trigger**: Competitive procurement >$250K with multiple proposals received
- **Reviewer**: Source Selection Authority + Evaluation Board (technical + cost + past performance)
- **Actions**: Independent evaluation, consensus scoring, competitive range determination, best-value tradeoff
- **Timeout**: Per procurement timeline (typically 30-60 days for evaluation)

### Bid Protest Response
- **Trigger**: Protest filed with GAO, Court of Federal Claims, or agency
- **Reviewer**: Contracting Officer + Agency Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Prepare agency report, produce documents, participate in ADR, implement decision
- **Timeout**: GAO: 100-day decision timeline; agency: 35 days for corrective action decision

name: Benefits Eligibility Determination
summary: Social services eligibility screening, determination, benefit calculation, and periodic recertification.
description:
A government benefits eligibility process managing 5,000-50,000 applications per month for programs including SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, and housing assistance. The process covers application intake (online, in-person, phone), identity/income verification, eligibility determination against program rules, benefit calculation, case issuance, and periodic redetermination.

**Key performance metrics:**
- Application processing timeliness: >95% within regulatory timeframes
- Determination accuracy: >97%
- Improper payment rate: <5%
- Customer wait time (in-office): <30 minutes
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **SNAP** (7 CFR 273) — 30-day processing, 7-day expedited for emergency
- **Medicaid** (42 CFR 435) — 45-day processing, 90-day for disability-based
- **TANF** state plan requirements and work participation rates
- **Privacy Act** and **state confidentiality** laws for applicant data

### Business Rules
- **Income verification** required for all applications (pay stubs, tax returns, employer verification)
- **Identity verification** through SAVE (immigration), SSA (Social Security), and state DMV interfaces
- **Expedited SNAP** processing within **7 days** for applicants with <$150 monthly income
- **Recertification** at program-defined intervals (6-12 months depending on program)

### SLA & Performance
- SNAP applications processed within **30 days** (7 days expedited)
- Medicaid applications processed within **45 days**
- Recertification notices mailed **45 days** before end of certification period
human_approvals:
### Complex Case Review
- **Trigger**: Mixed household, self-employment income, or multi-program interaction requiring manual adjudication
- **Reviewer**: Senior Eligibility Specialist + Supervisor
- **Actions**: Review documentation, apply complex income calculations, verify program interactions, determine eligibility
- **Timeout**: Within program-specific processing timeline (SNAP: 30 days, expedited: 7 days)

### Fair Hearing Request
- **Trigger**: Applicant/recipient disputes eligibility determination or benefit amount
- **Reviewer**: Hearing Officer (independent of determining office)
- **Actions**: Schedule hearing, prepare case file, present evidence, issue decision within regulatory timeline
- **Timeout**: Fair hearing within 90 days of request

name: Public Records & FOIA Processing
summary: FOIA and public records request intake, document search, legal review, redaction, and response.
description:
A government public records request process handling 500-5,000 FOIA/state public records requests per year. The process covers request intake, fee estimation, document search and collection, legal review for exemptions/redactions, response preparation, and appeal processing.

**Key performance metrics:**
- On-time response rate: >90% within statutory deadline
- Average processing time: <20 business days (simple), <60 days (complex)
- Appeal/litigation rate: <5%
- Proactive disclosure rate: increasing year-over-year
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **FOIA (5 USC §552)** — 20 business day response deadline (federal)
- **State public records laws** — varying deadlines (typically 5-30 days)
- **Privacy Act** — personal information protections in disclosed records
- **Presidential Records Act** for executive branch materials

### Business Rules
- All requests logged and tracked in **FOIA tracking system** within 24 hours of receipt
- **Multi-track processing**: simple (<100 pages), complex (>100 pages), expedited (compelling need)
- **Partial release** preferred over full withholding — segregable portions released
- **Requester communication** required every **20 business days** for complex requests

### SLA & Performance
- Simple requests: **20 business days**
- Complex requests: **60 business days** (with 10-day extension notification)
- Expedited requests: **10 business days** upon approval of expedited status
human_approvals:
### Exemption Application Review
- **Trigger**: Responsive documents contain potentially exempt information (privacy, law enforcement, trade secrets)
- **Reviewer**: FOIA Officer + Agency Legal Counsel
- **Actions**: Apply exemption analysis (foreseeable harm standard), prepare Vaughn index if needed, draft redaction justification
- **Timeout**: Within processing deadline — legal review prioritized

### Fee Waiver Request
- **Trigger**: Requester claims public interest fee waiver
- **Reviewer**: FOIA Officer
- **Actions**: Evaluate public interest criteria (subject of public concern, contribution to understanding, not primarily commercial)
- **Timeout**: 10 business days

name: Grant Management Lifecycle
summary: Federal/state grant application, award management, monitoring, reporting, and closeout per Uniform Guidance.
description:
A government grant management process handling 50-200 active grants totaling $50M-$500M across federal, state, and foundation funding sources. The process covers pre-award (application, budget development), award acceptance, implementation, fiscal management, programmatic/financial reporting, monitoring/audit, and closeout per 2 CFR 200 (Uniform Guidance).

**Key performance metrics:**
- Grant application success rate: >40%
- Expenditure rate vs. timeline: within 10% of plan
- Audit findings: 0 material findings
- Reporting on-time rate: >98%
- Indirect cost recovery rate: >95% of negotiated rate
business_constraints:
### Regulatory Requirements
- **2 CFR 200 (Uniform Guidance)** — cost principles, administrative requirements, audit requirements
- **Grantor-specific** terms and conditions
- **Single Audit Act** (annual audit for >$750K federal expenditure)
- **OMB Circulars** for cost allowability and allocation

### Business Rules
- **Prior approval** required for: equipment >$5K, foreign travel, subcontracts not in budget
- **Cost allocation** must be consistent, allowable, and allocable per 2 CFR 200 Subpart E
- **Progress reports** submitted per grant schedule (typically quarterly)
- **Grant closeout** within **120 days** of project end (final financial and programmatic reports)

### SLA & Performance
- Financial reports submitted by **grantor deadline** (typically 30-90 days post-period)
- Drawdowns processed within **5 business days** of request
- Subrecipient monitoring completed **annually**
human_approvals:
### Budget Modification Approval
- **Trigger**: Line item change >10% of total budget OR new budget category needed
